# Security Incident Update and Recommended Actions - Ultimate Member

Security Incident Update and Recommended Actions
================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**WPScan has confirmed that the vulnerability issue has been resolved in Ultimate Member Version 2.6.7. More information about this confirmation can be found at** [https://blog.wpscan.com/hacking-campaign-actively-exploiting-ultimate-member-plugin/.](https://blog.wpscan.com/hacking-campaign-actively-exploiting-ultimate-member-plugin/)

**To our Ultimate Member users,**

We have written this document to provide you with some additional information of the security vulnerabilities that were recently disclosed to us.

Firstly, we want to say sorry for these vulnerabilities in our plugin’s code and to any website that has been impacted and the worry this may have caused by learning of the vulnerabilities.

As soon as we were made aware that security vulnerabilities had been discovered in the plugin, we immediately began updating the code to patch the vulnerabilities.

We have released several updates since the disclosure as we worked through the vulnerabilities, and we want to say a big thank you to the team at WPScan for providing assistance and guidance with this after they got in touch to disclose the vulnerabilities.

Below is an overview of the events around this vulnerability as well as emphasizing additional security measures that we're implementing.

**Overview**

We received communication about a "A privilege escalation vulnerability" in the plugin on the 26th of June. We immediately started investigating and undertaking some internal testing whilst awaiting more details.

After receiving more details regarding the ability of attackers being able to use the 'wp\_capabiliti\\\\es' key we released **version 2.6.4,** which included a fix for that, and we also removed the extract() function from the admin-side of Ultimate Member plugin.

After this release, the WPScan team got in touch to let us know that attackers can still use the wp\_caPabilitiEs' key (metakey in the WP database is not case-sensitive). We released 2.6.5 to fix this and also removed the extract() function from the front-end of the plugin.

After this release, further communication with the WPScan team and the creation of this core trac ticket [https://core.trac.wordpress.org/ticket/58679#ticket](https://core.trac.wordpress.org/ticket/58679#ticket)
 we decided to change the submission handlers in the plugin

Marc Montpas (Automattic) wrote:

To be more clear, I'm not sure it can 100% be fixed without forbidding UTF-8 characters altogether, or, better, implementing an allowlist validation routine pattern to restrict form fields only to a set of known legitimate, rather than potential malicious metas. 

The problem is the "utf8mb4\_unicode\_ci" collation WordPress uses has a bunch of other character equivalence quirks like the ones I submitted earlier.  For example, another one that could bypass your latest fix are Unicode free-standing underscores, whose encoding follows the letter that needs to be underscored, instead of having one for each character in existence: 

user\_login-29=hackuser19&user\_password-29=P@ssw0rd%21&wP\_capa%cc%b2bilities-29\[administrator\]=1&confirm\_user\_password-29=P@ssw0rd%21&first\_name-29=Igor&form\_id=29&um\_request=&\_wpnonce=a918042906&\_wp\_http\_referer=%2fregister%2f  


There are hundreds of other side-cases like this, and as far as I know, WordPress does not have a function to "normalize" them all in a way that wouldn't cause other side-effects.

**Version 2.6.6** was released, which included hotfixes that had been detected after removing the extract function.

**Version 2.6.7** has been released on the 1st of July to patch the security vulnerability. 2.6.7 introduces whitelisting for metakeys which we store while sending forms. 2.6.7 also separates form settings data and submitted data and operates them in 2 different variables

**3rd Party modifications**

The release of 2.6.7 includes some significant changes to how forms submissions are handled. This may cause 3rd-party modifications to stop working. For Third-party developers, please update your customizations to support the new changes in the latest version. If you have a question or want to report a problem, please create a topic on the [forum](https://wordpress.org/support/plugin/ultimate-member/)
 or get in touch via our website. You may find the recent changes with the Ultimate Member [hooks in this site](https://ultimatemember.github.io/ultimatemember/hooks/index.html)
.

**Additional actions to take for your website and users**

As an additional security measure, we will shortly be releasing a feature within the plugin to enable the website admin to reset passwords for all users. The reason for this is a site using our plugin may have been hacked or injected with malware that sniffs login inputs, because this vulnerability issue is prone to these attacks, we recommend to reset passwords after updating with a security patch. This is to ensure the best protection for your website user’s passwords.

In the meantime, whilst we finalize testing on this new feature, we recommend that you follow the steps below to add additional security measures to your site.

*   **Review & Delete Unknown Administrator accounts** - The latest security vulnerability issue allows attackers to inject an administrator account to a WordPress site with Ultimate Member plugin prior to version 2.6.7. We recommend that you review all site administrators and delete those unknown accounts. 
*   **Reset All User Passwords (including your own admin account password)** - We recommend that you reset all user passwords and have users set a new password via the reset password mechanism. We will be introducing a feature in the plugin to enable this shortly. 
*   **Install & Active Security Plugins** - We recommend that you install Security plugins such as [WPScan](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wpscan/)
     or [WordFence](https://wordpress.org/plugins/wordfence/)
     to help your site detect suspicious activities. You may also try this plugin to help protect against XSS injections. 
*   **SSL Protection** - Ensure that your site runs on SSL certificates. SSL certificates will protect the sensitive data transmitted from and to your website. You can ask your hosting provider to assist you with setting up SSL on your server. 
*   **Daily Site Files & Database Backup** - It's one of the best practices to create daily backups of your site in case anything happens, you can restore your site to a stable version. 
*   **Send Advisories to your site members/customers about the incident** - We recommend that you inform your customers or site members about this incident and provide instructions on how to reset their passwords on your site. It's best to ask them to use a different password—not the same as their old password.  
      
      
    **W****hat’s next?**
We are awaiting feedback from WPScan on the version 2.6.7 release and are continuing to evaluate our plugin for other potential security vulnerabilities. We are also going to be evaluating all our extensions for increased security measures.

**Thank you**

We would like to apologize again for the stress and inconvenience this may have caused you and say thank you to everyone for your patience and understanding whilst we are working away on patching these issues. We are very lucky to have such a loyal user base of users with over 200,000 websites using our plugin, and ensuring the plugin is safe and secure to use is of the utmost importance to us. We have learned a lot from this recent security vulnerability disclosure and will be working hard to ensure the security of our plugin moving forward.

Last updated on July 10, 2023

No results found

# Installing the plugin from WordPress.org - Ultimate Member

Installing the plugin from WordPress.org
========================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Installing Ultimate Member is the same as installing any other WordPress plugin. Go to your WordPress “Plugins” page, from within WordPress admin dashboard, and do the following:

1.  Enter “Ultimate Member” into the search field and click “Search Plugins” or hit Enter.
2.  Select “Ultimate Member and click either “Details” or “Install Now”.
3.  Once Ultimate Member has been installed, click “Activate” to activate the plugin.

Ultimate Member is now activated and you should see the welcome screen.

Last updated on November 19, 2021

# Restrict content on a page/post to specific users community role - Ultimate Member

Restrict content on a page/post to specific users community role
================================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

With Ultimate Member, it is possible to exercise complete control over the visibility of content for specific user community roles. This is accomplished by encapsulating the content with the shortcode provided below:

\[um\_show\_content roles="member"\] This text can only be seen by users who have role "member" \[/um\_show\_content\]

It is essential to note that this feature offers a comprehensive solution for managing user community roles. The shortcode can be used to restrict access to specific content, making it invisible to certain user groups. This functionality provides an effective way to manage user permissions and ensure that only authorized individuals have access to sensitive information.

Ensure that the **Role ID** is utilized in the `roles` and `not` attributes for optimal performance. The Role ID is located in the Ultimate Member User Roles table, which can be found in the wp-admin>Ultimate Member> User Roles> Role ID.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6595094219a7f10eb7075a68/file-4CPYU6Z0mY.png)

### Show content to specific roles

\[um\_show\_content roles="member"\] <!-- insert content here --> \[/um\_show\_content\]

To add multiple target roles, simply separate each role with a comma ' **,**'.

\[um\_show\_content roles="subscriber,um\_moderator,um\_content-creator"\] <!-- insert content here --> \[/um\_show\_content\]

#### Example:

It's possible to restrict access to certain content based on the user role by adding the role IDs in the shortcode. This means that only users whose role IDs are **INCLUDED** in the shortcode will be able to view the content. The purpose of this feature is to ensure that the content is visible only to a specific group of users with particular roles, as defined by the shortcode. Therefore, it's crucial to select the appropriate role IDs while using the shortcode to control access to the content.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65951db3c14998364462eba7/file-G2S9I8y19M.png)

### Hide content from specific roles

\[um\_show\_content not="member"\] <!-- insert content here --> \[/um\_show\_content\]

To add multiple target roles, simply separate each role with a comma ' **,**'.

\[um\_show\_content not="subscriber,member"\] <!-- insert content here --> \[/um\_show\_content\]

#### Example:

The shortcode allows admins to restrict access to content by specifying the user role IDs. Content covered by the shortcode will be hidden or inaccessible to the specified user roles, allowing only **NON-INCLUDED** role IDs in the shortcode to view it. It's important to choose the correct IDs to regulate access.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/659525895843ce2dcf345b74/file-12YSBY11hn.png)

### Adding tags to text

[Common placeholders (tags)](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1340-placeholders-for-email-templates)
 can be used inside the shortcode. The shortcode then converts these to their proper values.

\[um\_show\_content roles="contributor,subscriber"\] {display\_name} \[/um\_show\_content\]

#### What the specific roles see:

The content that is restricted on the page/post is hidden from other community roles. They won't see any indication that there is content they cannot access.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/659580a1a55da02676abff75/file-ymgrYLALvO.png)

Last updated on April 22, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [Ultimate Member - Shortcodes](/article/210-ultimate-member-shortcodes)
    
*   [Restrict content on a page/post to logged in users](/article/177-restrict-content-on-a-page-post-to-logged-in-users)
    
*   [Restrict content on a page/post to logged out users](/article/176-restrict-content-on-a-page-post-to-logged-out-users)

# Installing an Extension - Ultimate Member

Installing an Extension
=======================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

To make use of any of our Ultimate Member Extensions, you need to ensure that the Ultimate Member core plugin is active and then install and activate the extensions. The process is similar to installing a regular plugin.

Downloading Extensions
----------------------

1.  You can download available extensions through your account on our website. Visit [https://ultimatemember.com/](https://ultimatemember.com/)
    , click the **"Sign In"** button, and log in using the email you used during checkout.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/661420985b008e1c56a9900d/file-hPGgkNEuIc.png)
    
2.  Once logged in, you'll be redirected to your [Account page](https://ultimatemember.com/account/)
    .
    1.  For **individual extension purchases**, download the extension from the **"File Downloads"** tab. ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6614236a477c990280e43430/file-PeYZWWDVeo.png)
    2.  For **extension pass purchases**, access the downloads page via the **"Purchases"** tab and click **"View Details and Downloads."![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66142496ef6cd1080a2eaa05/file-ltsnZSFTuF.png)**After clicking, you'll be redirected to the purchase confirmation page. Under the Pages section, click on the **"Downloads"** link to proceed to the **"My Downloads"** Page.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/661428e6477c990280e43437/file-ZnRqekx8DG.png)

### Installing Downloaded Extensions

1.  To install downloaded extensions, navigate to your WordPress dashboard, then go to **Plugins > Add New.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6616f40cdac5c26585fc624e/file-pymLy615AU.png)**
2.  Look for the **"Upload Plugin"** button. Click it, then click **"Choose File"** to select the ZIP file of your downloaded extension from your computer.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6616f466dac5c26585fc624f/file-mR8kWgQNhU.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6616f5a2d9cffa6c1049d337/file-Z32qpXvoHv.png)
    
3.  After selecting the file, click **"Install Now"**. Once the installation is complete, click **"Activate Plugin"**. 
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6616f8994db6eb7a51d93557/file-xezIkXpmPZ.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6616f8a74db6eb7a51d93558/file-dW9ZcbbBCJ.png)
    
4.  You should see a message confirming the activation. To verify, scroll down to the installed extension. If you see a **"Deactivate"** link, it means the plugin is activated.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6616f9d2ef6cd1080a2eac48/file-LYjRSsPPYR.png)

### Entering Your License Key

1.  A license key is necessary for receiving updates. Obtain your License Key from your account on [ultimatemember.com](https://ultimatemember.com/account/)
    .
2.  Enter this key on the page **wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Licenses** or click the Licenses page link from the notice.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6616fc4a477c990280e43660/file-kyiW6cwON8.png)
    

For detailed instructions, refer to our article on  [**How to activate a license key**](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1766-how-to-activate-license-key)
.

Last updated on April 10, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [Installing the plugin from WordPress.org](/article/26-installing-plugin-from-wordpress-org)

# Creating Plugin Core Pages Manually - Ultimate Member

Creating Plugin Core Pages Manually
===================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

This article presents a detailed, step-by-step guide on manually creating core pages. Normally, these pages are automatically generated when you click the **"Create Pages"** button in the notice after installing and activating the Ultimate Member core plugin, as described in this [article](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/111-default-pages)
. Before proceeding to create the six default pages, it's advisable to review the following articles:

*   [Default Pages](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/111-default-pages)
    
*   [Pages Tab](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/38-pages-tab)
    
*   [Core Pages Shortcodes](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/210-ultimate-member-shortcodes#pages)
    

If the button fails to generate the pages and you decide to create them manually, follow the steps below:

1.  **Register Page**
    *   Go to wp-admin > Pages and click on the **'Add New Page'** button.
    *   Add the page title **'Register'**.
    *   In the content area, [add or paste the default registration form shortcode](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/210-ultimate-member-shortcodes#login)
        . You can locate the shortcode for the **default registration form** in the shortcode column within wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65ccc5db1515e26e4a2f4d61/file-Dcq8hyk5vc.png)
    *   Click the **'Publish'** button to save the page.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65ccfd401359983245253b78/file-XoJboVqunR.png)
2.  **Login Page**
    *   Go to wp-admin > Pages and click on the **'Add New Page'** button.
    *   Add the page title **'Login'.**
    *   In the content area, add the [default login form shortcode](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/210-ultimate-member-shortcodes#login)
        . You can locate the shortcode for the **default login form** in the shortcode column within wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65ccc7b01409b478491f5f1e/file-Y3NmY3XBjf.png)
    *   Click the **'Publish'** button to save the page.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65ccfbf51359983245253b77/file-JFC1itqu5P.png)
3.  **User Page**
    *   Go to wp-admin > Pages and click on the **'Add New Page'** button.
    *   Add the page title **'User'.**
    *   In the content area, add the [default profile form shortcode](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/210-ultimate-member-shortcodes#login)
        . You can locate the shortcode for the **default profile form** in the shortcode column within wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65ccf9ec1409b478491f5f64/file-jSWxNpTv1J.png)
    *   Click the **'Publish'** button to save the page.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65ccfca12cb21c6ca90d9845/file-jObQ5bzlrm.png)
4.  **Account Page**
    *   Go to wp-admin > Pages and click on the **'Add New Page'** button.
    *   Add the page title **'Account'.**
    *   In the content area, add the [account form shortcode](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/210-ultimate-member-shortcodes#account)
        :`[ultimatemember_account]`.
    *   Click the **'Publish'** button to save the page.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65ccffd31409b478491f5f6f/file-N0Ewa6wpwN.png)
5.  **Members Page**
    *   Go to wp-admin > Pages and click on the **'Add New Page'** button.
    *   Add the page title **'Members'**.
    *   In the content area, add the [default member directory shortcode](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/210-ultimate-member-shortcodes#members)
        . You can locate the shortcode for the **default member directory** in the shortcode column within wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Member Directories.
    *   ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65cd04202cb21c6ca90d9851/file-oEKi4gKCM5.png)Click the **'Publish'** button to save the page.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65cd0460c5625b1953dde21c/file-pZcvhMLpZu.png)
6.  **Logout Page**
    *   Go to wp-admin > Pages and click on the **'Add New Page'** button.
    *   Add the page title **'Logout'**.
    *   Click the **'Publish'** button to save the page.
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65cd05741409b478491f5f78/file-XcnIhcpOp1.png)
7.  **Password Reset**
    *   Go to wp-admin > Pages and click on the **'Add New Page'** button.
    *   Add the page title **'Password Reset'.**
    *   In the content area, add the [password reset shortcode](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/210-ultimate-member-shortcodes#passwordReset)
        : `[ultimatemember_password]`
    *   Click the **'Publish'** button to save the page.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65cd08491515e26e4a2f4dcd/file-p9NY5Tx6Zs.png)

After manually creating all the necessary core pages, the final step is crucial. You must assign them in **WP Admin > UM > Settings > General** **\> Pages**. Select the created page on your site from the drop-down menu and save the changes for that page to be assigned to the UM core element. Then, save the settings by clicking on the **'Save changes'** button.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65cd0f061409b478491f5f88/file-zO0C2ylQCl.png)

Last updated on February 15, 2024

No results found

# Common content restriction settings - Ultimate Member

Common content restriction settings
===================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

Ultimate Member's **Content Restriction** allows you to manage site visibility for everyone or logged-in users globally, customize per-page or per-post access rules, and define specific restrictions and redirect behaviors for both logged-in and logged-out users, including the option to lock the homepage for certain user roles. This guide details the configurations to control access to the site and pages, which can be located at **wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Access > Restriction Content.**

Global Site Access
------------------

The Global Site Access feature provides that let you the flexibility to define the accessibility of your entire site with two primary options:

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/657193eecdc01d716a36e595/file-85uhsg4Hjd.png)

*   ### Site accessible to Everyone:
    
    *   This is the **default Global Site Access setting**, allowing everyone, including non-members or users, to view all pages and posts on your site.
    *   Use this option to permit non-members to access your site's content while restricting certain pages to members only.
    *   The illustration below demonstrates that everyone, including logged-out users, can view the Members Page. ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6571cf7b2102cf51ddb7fee9/file-8cppSscZKv.png)
    
    In this example, an attempt was made to restrict only the Member Page, while the global access is set to **"Site accessible to Everyone."** As a result, the Member Page becomes restricted while other pages remain accessible to everyone.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/657caf56e020fe4b2fcaf109/file-9t1qmvoQSK.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6571c6e518590a2d69aaa076/file-H0QmHjO4Tk.png)
    
*   ### Site accessible to Logged in users:
    
    **Note: Pages such as Login, Registration, and Password Reset cannot be restricted and are exceptions.**
    
    *   Choosing this option limits access to logged-in users only. Every page or post on your site will be available solely to users who are logged in unless you change the access rule on a particular page or post to make a page available to everyone.
    *   If this option is selected, additional settings will be available:
        *   **Custom Redirect URL**: Redirects logged-out users attempting to access restricted content to the specified URL.
        *   **Exclude the following URLs**: Excludes specified URLs from content restrictions, allowing them to be accessible to everyone.
        *   **Allow Homepage to be accessible**: Grants access to the homepage for everyone if enabled.
        *   **Allow Category pages to be accessible**: Permits access to category pages for everyone if enabled.

However, it's crucial to create a designated page for non-members or logged-out users to sign up or log in when using this option. For instance, you may consider making your homepage a landing page to encourage new users to sign up. If a logged-out user attempts to access a restricted page, they will be redirected to the homepage or landing page. In the example below, we set the custom redirect URL to the login page URL. So, when a logged-out user visits a restricted page (e.g., Members page), they are redirected to the login page.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6571ed1118590a2d69aaa0a0/file-yxlKZxl4WL.png)

Replace the restricted post title
---------------------------------

This option allows setting the restricted post's title for the content that isn't accessible to the visitor or current user.

*   ### Restricted Access Post Title
    

This is the title shown to users who do not have permission to view the post. It affects the individual post page, archive post in a widget, or post link in the nav menu titles. The default title is " **Restricted content**"

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6576f4c0341632179e63685e/file-qN0x8kKlpQ.png)

Restricted Access Message[#](#global)

--------------------------------------

This message will be shown to users who do not have permission to view the content, this will be the global default message. It can be edited visually or as HTML text.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6576f9741034bb3b9b9f8bd0/file-OeEqfrvR9j.png)

### Enable the "Content Restriction" settings for the Gutenberg Blocks

This enables the admin to set the restriction settings for Gutenberg Blocks. If enabled,the admin can enable or disable content restrictions for the Gutenberg blocks in each post.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65770bf862af430bbf238387/file-7fNUX7GYyR.png)

*   ### Restricted Access Block Message
    

This message will be shown to users who do not have permission to view the Gutenberg block.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6577385a4f53e13afbf2ef67/file-yOy9iojd9S.png)

*   If the admin has selected **Show global default message** under the Restriction action in edit post/page > Block settings, the restricted access block message is displayed to logged-out users.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65772fce4f53e13afbf2ef50/file-cqK2cPEiyp.png)
    

How it looks in the front end: ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/657736ed62af430bbf2383d4/file-wP9mBuLyaZ.png)

*   Logged-out users are not able to access or view the restricted Gutenberg block if the admin has selected **'Hide block'** under the Restriction action.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65770c144f37be75a5026cfa/file-lOFbhTu6Dh.png)

How it looks in the front end:

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65770dd662af430bbf238389/file-zlZXcjJLFg.png)

### Enable the "Content Restriction" settings for post types

This enables the "Content Restriction" feature for the individual posts of the selected post types.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65773d234f53e13afbf2ef71/file-hNV7cc2czm.png)

*   If enabled, the admin can enable or disable content restriction of a post while editing, and they can also choose who can access the post with the following options: **Everyone, Logged out users, Logged in users**. Individual post-restriction settings are described [here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1623-post-term-restriction-content-settings)
    .

### Enable the "Content Restriction" settings for taxonomies

This enables the "Content Restriction" feature for the individual terms of the selected taxonomies.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65773d474f53e13afbf2ef72/file-JgCvRqnqMS.png)

*   If enabled, the admin can manage content restriction of a taxonomy while editing. Individual taxonomy restriction settings are described [here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1623-post-term-restriction-content-settings)
    .

Last updated on January 5, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [Access Tab](/article/41-access-tab)
    
*   [Individual Post and Term content restriction settings](/article/1623-post-term-restriction-content-settings)
    
*   [Restriction content priority](/article/1620-restriction-content)
    

No results found

# User Tags + Member Directories 2.1.0+ - Ultimate Member

User Tags + Member Directories 2.1.0+
=====================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

There are two options to customize links for the displaying users by the tag and general organic search by the user tag in member directories.

Settings:

*   **User Tag slug** - this option allows you to set the user tags' taxonomy slug, which is used in rewrite rules.
*   **Base member directory** - this option allows you to select what member directory is used for the displaying users by the tag at the page

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/640475b53c396c395ec0f8ff/file-gK2YOhz0oh.png)

A tag link structure: `{members-page-url}/{user-tag-slug}/{term-slug}/?tag_field={user-tag-field-key}` where `{user-tag-slug}` is the **User Tag slug** setting.

A tag link example: `https://site.local/members/user-tag/blogging/?tag_field=interests` where `user-tag` is the **User Tag slug** value.

User tags shown in profile are links that navigate to the member directory with predefined filtering by chosen user tag.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6415d04301b8d2001d5202ff/file-QLWA9y79yo.png)

Last updated on March 18, 2023

# Social Login Common Issues - Ultimate Member

Social Login Common Issues
==========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**Version 2.3+**

The Social Login extension uses 1-step and  2-step processes for the first time registrations. When a user clicks on a social network button to register they will be redirected to the social network site to confirm permissions and then return back to your website's previous page URL as Redirect a URI of your apps where a social overlay should show which includes the social registration form( for 2-step process ) so users can complete the registration.

**1\. Social Overlay/Fields not showing up**

If you do not see the overlay or fields on it when you are returned to your site and instead just see a white overlay page, please go to your registration form  **WP-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Your registration form** and turn ON "**Use this form in the overlay**" this will show your form in the overlay.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5b17bccb0428632c466a9935/file-HIEhbVPpvd.jpg)

**2\. Getting Redirect URI Mismatch Errors**

If you're getting an SDK/API error or Mismatch Redirect URI errors, please read the common issues and solutions below:

a .Your hosting provider has a \`register\_globals\` that's turned on. Please ask your hosting provider to turn it off. Once the hosting provider has confirmed this is off, please check the issue again.

b. API callback and site URL settings doesn't match the current site domain and its protocol ( http or https ). Also, check if your site URL returns www or non-www that could cause mismatch Redirect URIs set in your Apps.

c. You entered Invalid API credentials in the Ultimate Member social connects settings.

d. There's a Javascript error that's affecting the overlay script. Please check the browser's console to check for any errors from the theme and other plugins.

e. There's a caching plugin or varnish/memcache installed on your host. We recommend to exclude those pages where Social Login buttons are added. Also, try clearing the cache/memcache after your install the Social Login extension and pages.

f. Check for broken links or images that causing a 404 page. It duplicates the authentication request that causes 'state' doesn't match.

g. Your theme/plugin has incompatibility issues with Social Login's Sessions. We recommend that you do a [theme/plugin conflict test](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/96-how-to-do-a-plugin-theme-conflict-test)
.

**3\. Unable to Request App Access Permissions**

If users are getting the message **"We were unable to request application access permissions."** on app authentication,  there's a possible cache issue with your site/server. If you have a Varnish Cache or Cache plugin installed on your site/server, please try excluding the following cookie from the cache: **PHPSESSID**

**4\. Looping Register Form on submission after Request Application Access Permissions**

*   If you're using the One-Step Process option, you need to remove the following fields from the Register Form:
    *   ReCaptcha
    *   Terms & Conditions / Privacy Policy
    *   Password Fields
*   Fields should be synced with the SSO fields in the field settings: ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5e624cd32c7d3a7e9ae8c84e/file-kShCDJQg6T.png) 

Last updated on June 14, 2023

No results found

# Adding Existing Fields from Register Form to Profile Form - Ultimate Member

Adding Existing Fields from Register Form to Profile Form
=========================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

When users enter information in the Registration form during the sign-up process, it is essential to ensure that this data can be seamlessly displayed and edited in the Profile form. To achieve this, you must include the same fields in both forms.

**Important Note:** Ultimate Member does not automatically add fields to the `wp-admin > Users > Profile` screen.

### Example Implementation

In the example below, we will integrate the predefined fields _**First Name**_ and _**Last Name**_, along with a custom field titled _**Position**_, into both the Registration and Profile forms. This approach can be extended to other fields as needed.

1.  **Fields in the Registration Form:**
    *   [Add the predefined fields](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/188-how-to-add-fields-to-a-form)
         _First Name_, _Last Name_, and the custom field _Position_ to the Registration form.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc38a4dd5b956281ae59af/file-GnLEvyrVWg.png)
    *   During registration, users will enter their first name, last name, and work position. This information will be securely saved after their registration.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc3955dd5b956281ae59b1/file-020V3ktk4R.png)
2.  **Fields in the Profile Form:**
    *   To display this information in the Profile form, navigate to `wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms`, and edit the Profile form.
    *   Add the _First Name_ and _Last Name_ fields in the Form Builder section.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc3d4b44628317ef90485f/file-iYRI8F4Yz1.png)
    *   For any custom fields you have created, locate them in the **"Custom Fields"** section within the Fields Manager.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc3bf5811a2434cb6d4628/file-AVT6Us9TZT.png)
3.  **Final Steps:**
    *   After adding the necessary fields, click the **Update** button to save your changes.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc3c828af27b34842bf6b8/file-nw7iQ9e06V.png)
    *   The user's first name, last name, and work position will now be displayed in their profile. If the fields are configured as editable, users can modify this information directly in their profiles by clicking on the gear icon and selecting **Edit Profile**.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc407b811a2434cb6d462b/file-mLgip4MEbO.png)

### Common Issues When Adding Fields

**Issue:** You may encounter the following error message when trying to add or create a field:

*   _"Your meta key already exists in your fields list."  
    _

**Resolution:** These messages indicate that the meta key you are attempting to use already exists in the system. To resolve this:

*   **Check the Fields Manager:** Navigate to _Form Builder > Add a field "+" > Custom Field_. Hover over the custom fields to view their meta keys. If the field and its meta key already exist, consider using a predefined field from the modal instead of creating a duplicate.
*   **Modify Your Custom Field's Meta Key:** If you must create a new custom field, ensure that its meta key is unique. This will prevent conflicts and allow for proper data integration.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc40af811a2434cb6d462c/file-qeF8iByETP.png)
    

### Conclusion

By effectively utilizing both predefined and custom fields, you can enhance the user experience on your site and ensure data consistency across forms. Always keep track of existing meta keys to avoid duplication errors, and refer to the provided documentation for specific use cases and advanced configurations.

Last updated on October 1, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [How to Add Fields to a Form](/article/188-how-to-add-fields-to-a-form)
    
*   [Form Builder](/article/85-form-builder)
    

No results found

# Ultimate Member - Search Users - Ultimate Member

Ultimate Member - Search Users
==============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Overview

**Search Users** shortcode provides instant search results in the member directory as you type in its search field. By default, this shortcode is used by **Ultimate Member - Search** widget.

![](https://content.screencast.com/users/NikitaSinelnikov7357/folders/Jing/media/f21a26de-0d1b-4d4b-b0b1-cf2961341c95/2019-10-23_1246.png)

Use this shortcode anywhere on your site to allow customers to find the users via the general search field.

\[ultimatemember\_searchform /\]

Image - How to add the  **\[ultimatemember\_searchform /\]** shortcode to a page.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/63834fddeb3e4a7280a2bd14/file-38CVUog4LF.png)

You should see a simple block with an input and the "Search" button (icon) on the front. Type a keyword and click the "Search" button. The site will redirect you to the Members page and show users who have a keyword in their data.

Image - A view of the **\[ultimatemember\_searchform/\]** shortcode on the page.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/638350038ae44905ae2ccae4/file-y6UyiUErWc.png)

The searching form is hidden if the Members page is disabled or the searching feature is disabled. Turn on the **Enable Members Directory** setting on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > General > Users_ and turn on the **Enable Search feature** setting on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Member Directories > Edit Member Directory_ to enable the search form.

**Warning:** Make sure that you have installed the Members page [here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/111-default-pages)
 and there is a shortcode for the member directory, which has the general search bar. More information about member directory settings is [here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1513-member-directories-2-1-0#search)
.

**Note:** If there is more than 1 shortcode with the active search bar on the members' directory page, a search will be performed for all shortcodes that have a general search bar display setting.

# Choices Callback Feature in UM 2.1+ - Ultimate Member

Choices Callback Feature in UM 2.1+
===================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Overview

This document provides information on how to set up the **Choices Callback** feature to make a specific dropdown field and the **Parent Option** feature to display different array of options depending on the user choice in the parent field.

> This article is actual for the Ultimate Member 2.1 and next versions. See this [article](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1486-how-to-use-choices-callback)
>  for older versions.

### Custom function

The **Choices Callback** value is a name of your custom function that returns an array of options for the field. Array keys are used as option names and array values are used as option titles. You can add your custom functions to the _functions.php_ file in the theme directory.

### Examples

#### Example 1 - Make a specific dropdown field

Add the custom function `custom_country_choices_callback` to the _functions.php_ file. Add a custom dropdown field **Country** to your form and paste the function name "custom\_country\_choices\_callback" to the field's **Choices Callback** setting.

function custom\_country\_choices\_callback() {

	// choices array.
	$options = array(
		"FR" => "France",
		"ES" => "Spain",
		"IT" => "Italy",
	);

	return array\_unique( $options );
}

Image - The custom **Country** field settings.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/647f0da4936c7945969ed90c/file-Ywqvz0a0zX.png)

#### Example 2 - Make a specific dropdown field that displays different options depending on the parent field

Add the custom function `custom_city_choices_callback` to the _functions.php_ file. Add a custom dropdown field **City** to your form and paste the function name "custom\_city\_choices\_callback" to the field's **Choices Callback** setting. Select the **Country** field in the **Parent Option** setting to link these fields.

Here is a function that you can use to get cities for France (FR) and Spain (ES). If you select any of these countries in the **Country** field, cities will be show up as options in the **City** field.

function custom\_city\_choices\_callback() {

	// choices array.
	$all\_options = array(
		"FR" => array(
			"Paris"     => "Paris",
			"Marseille" => "Marseille",
			"Lyon"      => "Lyon"
		),
		"ES" => array(
			"Madrid"    => "Madrid",
			"Barcelona" => "Barcelona"
		),
	);

	// get values from the parent field, sent via the AJAX post.
	$parent  = empty( $\_POST\['parent\_option'\] ) ? '' : sanitize\_text\_field( $\_POST\['parent\_option'\] );
	$options = ( $parent && array\_key\_exists( $parent, $all\_options ) ) ? $all\_options\[ $parent \] : array();

	return array\_unique( $options );
}

Image - The custom **City** field settings.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/647f0f3e95b6b2126f5265a9/file-5kOGbhanvp.png)

Image - Two specific dropdown fields where the **City** field options depend on the choice in the **Country** field.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5ca1cf242c7d3a154461999b/file-a3DKAsxyYz.jpg)

#### Example 3 - Four related dropdown fields linked using the Parent Option setting

This example is too big for an article, see the code and screenshots on [GitHubGist](https://gist.github.com/yuriinalivaiko/0a804d322f7ff19fc2594a1e029ae8e7)
.

Last updated on December 6, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [How to use choices callback feature](/article/1486-how-to-use-choices-callback)
    
*   [Dynamic Country-State-City Dependent Dropdown fields](/article/1884-custom-callback-dropdowns)

# Conditional Logic - Ultimate Member

Conditional Logic
=================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

Ultimate Member provides conditional logic within the form builder which allows you to configure your registration, login, or profile form to show or hide fields based on user selections. Conditional logic can be applied to a field when creating a new field or editing an existing or pre-defined field.

Adding conditional logic to a field [#](#adding_conditions)

------------------------------------------------------------

To add conditional logic to a field you need to open up an individual field’s options by adding a new field from the field modal or clicking the edit icon to edit an existing field. Near the bottom of the field modal, you will see text that says: “Add conditional fields support”. Clicking on this text will open the conditional logic options.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5620f7de903360610fc68cb3/file-lW5wQMzmlo.png)

### Adding a condition [#](#condition)

Thick the "+" icon to add one more condition. Each condition is a line with inputs: Action, Field selection, Operator, Value.

#### Action [#](#condition_action)

The basic idea with conditional logic is that you show or hide a field based on the user’s selection of another field. If you select the  **show**, the field will only appear on the form once the conditions are met. If you select **hide**, the field will disappear from the field once the conditions are met.

#### Field selection [#](#condition_switcher)

The second input required for conditional logic is to select the field which will trigger the conditional logic to show/hide. Click the dropdown and select the name of the field from the list. Only fields that have been added to the form are shown in this dropdown.

**Warning:** Conditional logic is strictly hierarchical. Cross dependencies are not allowed. This means that you can make field B depend on field A or field A depend on field B. But if you make field B depend on field A and field A depend on field B, it will cause an error.

#### Operator [#](#condition_operator)

The third input required for conditional logic is to select the operator that will trigger the conditional logic. There are several operators to choose from:

*   **Empty** – this field will show/hide if the selected field is empty (e.g. has no user input)
*   **Not Empty** – this field will show/hide if the selected field is not empty (e.g. the field has user input)
*   **Equals to** – this field will show/hide if the selected field is equal to the text or number you set in the value input (e.g if you set the value input to be London and the user selects London from the country list then the field will show/hide)
*   **Not equals** – this field will show/hide if the selected field is not equal to the text or number you set in the value input (e.g if you set the value input to be London and the user selects New York from the country list then the field will show/hide)
*   **Greater than** – this field will show/hide if the selected field is greater than the number you set in the value input (e.g has no user input)
*   **Less than** – this field will show/hide if the selected field is greater than the number you set in the value input (e.g has no user input)
*   **contains** – this field will show/hide if the selected field contains the text or number you set in the value input. This operator should be used if the selected field type is _"Checkbox", "Dropdown", "Multi-Select" or "Radio"_ - these field types save data as an array.

#### Value [#](#condition_value)

The final input required for conditional logic is to set the value. This involves entering the text or number that will trigger the field to show/hide. Below is what you can enter into the value input for each operator type:

*   **Empty** – this operator requires no value input and should be left with the default 0
*   **Not Empty** – this operator requires no value input and should be left with the default 0
*   **Equals to** – the value may be either text or number. If entering text make sure the text matches exactly with what the user selects (e.g if the user selects Male from the gender field make sure you set the value as Male and not male).
*   **Not equals** – the value may be either text or number. If entering text make sure the text matches exactly with the what the user selects (e.g if the user selects Male from the gender field make sure you set the value as Male and not male).
*   **Greater than** – this operator requires that the value be a number, not text.
*   **Less than** – this operator requires that the value be a number, not text.
*   **contains** – the value must be the same as one of the choices in the field-switcher. For example, your field-switcher contains choices "first item", "second item", "third item". You should use one of these values. Values "first", "item" or "first item second item" would not work.

### Adding multiple conditions [#](#multiple_conditions)

It is possible to add more than one condition to each field. You are able to add up to five conditions per field. This means you have more flexibility over what conditions will make a field show/hide on the form.

Conditional fields don't use "AND" or "OR" logic. Conditional logic is based on form actions. The related field will be changed (shown or hidden) when the value of the field-switcher is changed. That's why we recommend using the combination "single switcher - multiple related fields" and don't recommend using combination "multiple switchers - single related field".

You should add several rules to the related field's conditional logic if you want to show related fields for several choices of the field-switcher.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5f39581a042863444aa0622a/file-fTMt8qX2Ec.png)

### Exceptions [#](#exceptions)

#### Roles [#](#exceptions_roles)

You may use conditional logic with the field types "Roles (Dropdown)" and "Roles (Radio)". In this case, you should use these settings to manage conditional fields:

*   Action - "show"
*   Field - select the field-switcher;
*   Operator - "contains";
*   Value - the **Role ID** of the user role in the field-switcher. Look at the table on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles_ to find proper **Role ID**.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5f3e868d2c7d3a352e9120e5/file-UmQo97PhMP.png)

#### User Tags [#](#exceptions_user_tags)

You may use conditional logic with the field type "User Tags". In this case, you should use these settings to manage conditional fields:

*   Action - "show"
*   Field - select the field-switcher;
*   Operator - "contains";
*   Value - the ID number of the target tag in the field-switcher.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5f3a7b55042863444aa06850/file-89VNdxM7z7.png)

Last updated on January 6, 2025

No results found

# Default WordPress Fields Limit - Ultimate Member

Default WordPress Fields Limit
==============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Ultimate Member allows you to add or create custom fields that you can add to your forms, but you need to consider the default field limits based on the WordPress database.  If you input data that is over the limit, it will not be saved. 

The following are the default field character limits:

*   **user\_login** - this field accepts **60** characters.
*   **user\_pass** - this field is encrypted that accepts **255** characters.
*   **user\_nicename** - this field is used for the friendly URL of the user's page and it accepts **50** characters.
*   **user\_email** - this field is for the user's email it accepts **100** characters.
*   **user\_url** \- this field is where you can input the user's URL. It accepts **100** characters.
*   **display\_name**  - this field is for the user's display name, it accepts **250** characters.

Data that have longer characters than the fields limit above, you can create another field with another meta key in the field settings under form builder.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61d99699d268f00e10386c05/file-82hANqLock.png)

Last updated on January 8, 2022

No results found

# Dynamic Country-State-City Dependent Dropdown fields - Ultimate Member

Dynamic Country-State-City Dependent Dropdown fields
====================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

This document provides the code necessary to populate custom callback dropdowns for Country, State, and City fields. We recommend reading this [article](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1539-choices-callback-feature-in-um-2-1)
 thoroughly to understand the choices callback feature in detail. For implementing dynamic, dependent dropdowns for these fields, you can use the **Country State City Dropdown (Pro)** plugin. This third-party plugin integrates seamlessly with Ultimate Member forms, enabling auto-population of options based on user selections and facilitating a smooth setup with the callback functions below.

To begin, you should add the provided code snippet located at the end of this article to your theme's functions.php file or utilize the Code Snippets plugin.

#### **Custom Country Dropdown Field Settings**

In your **custom country** dropdown field setting, paste in the **Choices Callback** option the function name   `um_customcb_populate_countries` into the field's **"Choices Callback"** setting.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/653000421ec94935420480f7/file-5GIalt9Szb.png)

#### **Custom State Dropdown Field Settings**

In your **custom state** dropdown field setting, paste in the **Choices Callback** option the function name `um_customcb_populate_states`  into the field's **"Choices Callback"** setting. Select the **country** field in the **Parent Option** setting to link these fields.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6530063674982009a94e7614/file-deZ9OOf6FI.png)

#### Custom City Dropdown Field Settings

In your  **custom city** dropdown field setting, paste in the Choices Callback option the function name `um_customcb_populate_city`  into the field's **"Choices Callback"** setting. Select the **state** field in the Parent Option setting to link these fields.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65300abb74982009a94e761c/file-6pmH6RX7PV.png)

**WARNING:** We have created this code example to provide guidance and to make it easier for you to implement this code into your website. However, we are not able to provide any support when it comes to customizing the plugin. If you need help implementing this code, please hire a developer.

**Note:** You can add the code snippet to your theme's functions.php file or use the Code Snippets plugin.

Last updated on November 4, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [Choices Callback Feature in UM 2.1+](/article/1539-choices-callback-feature-in-um-2-1)

# Fields Validation - Ultimate Member

Fields Validation
=================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

There are several ways to validate a field value:

1.  Select a predefined validation option in the field setting **Validate**.
2.  Select the **Custom Validation** option in the **Validate** setting, then type a keyword for the custom validation hook in the **Custom Action** setting. Attach your custom validation function to the hook `um_custom_field_validation_{custom_action}`, where `{custom_action}` is a keyword in the **Custom Action** setting.
3.  Attach your custom validation function to the hook `um_submit_form_errors_hook_`.

Note: You can add your custom PHP code to the _functions.php_ file in the active theme directory.

Validation options
------------------

A list of predefined validation rules:

*   Alphabetic value only
*   Google URL
*   VK URL
*   Alpha-numeric value
*   English letters only
*   Facebook URL
*   Instagram URL
*   LinkedIn URL
*   Lowercase only
*   Numeric value only
*   Phone Number
*   Skype ID
*   SoundCloud Profile
*   Twitter URL
*   E-mail( Not Unique )
*   Unique E-mail
*   Unique Metakey value
*   Unique Username
*   Unique Username/E-mail
*   Website URL
*   YouTube Profile
*   Telegram URL
*   Discord ID
*   **Custom Validation**

Image - Field settings, predefined validation options.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6422e44a5d17363e673bdfa8/file-QVoDyGL3ax.png)

Custom Validation option
------------------------

You can select the **Custom Validation** option in the **Validate** setting and add your custom validation function to the _functions.php_ file in the active theme directory. Attach your custom validation function to the hook `um_custom_field_validation_{custom_action}`, where `{custom_action}` is a keyword in the **Custom Action** setting.

Image - Field settings.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6422e68669becd1202b9f2a2/file-P0QGbSw1OD.png)

Code example - Custom validation function attached to the custom action hook.

function um\_custom\_validate\_mobile\_number( $key, $array, $args ) {
	if ( isset( $args\[$key\] ) && !preg\_match('/^\[6-9\]\\d{9}$/', $args\[$key\]) ) {
		UM()->form()->add\_error( $key, \_\_( 'Please enter valid Mobile Number.', 'ultimate-member' ) );
	}
}
add\_action( 'um\_custom\_field\_validation\_mobile\_number', 'um\_custom\_validate\_mobile\_number', 30, 3 );

Image - Error message in the form.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6422e6a55b17ef2714cec668/file-aXhQiY1f1m.png)

Hook um\_submit\_form\_errors\_hook\_
-------------------------------------

You can use the `um_submit_form_errors_hook_` hook to apply custom validations and rules for any fields and stop forms from being submitted until the conditions are met.

In the example below we check that the username field does not have the word "admin".

Code example - Custom validation function attached to the hook.

function um\_custom\_validate\_username( $args ) {	
	if ( isset( $args\['user\_login'\] ) && strstr( $args\['user\_login'\], 'admin' ) ) {
		UM()->form()->add\_error( 'user\_login', 'Your username must not contain the word admin.' );
	}
}
add\_action('um\_submit\_form\_errors\_hook\_','um\_custom\_validate\_username', 999, 1);

Custom Validating with two fields
---------------------------------

Examples below will help you validate and compare two fields. We use "user\_login" & "nickname" as examples. The user will get an error message if the values in both fields are the same.

### Example with the Custom Validation option

Image - Field settings

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6422e8375b17ef2714cec669/file-9bEfdZOdLA.png)

Code example - Custom validation function attached to the custom action hook.

function um\_custom\_validate\_username\_nickname( $key, $array, $args ) {
	if ( isset( $args\[$key\] ) && isset( $args\['user\_login'\] ) && $args\[$key\] == $args\['user\_login'\] ) {
		UM()->form()->add\_error( $key, \_\_( 'Your username and nickname can not be equal.', 'ultimate-member' ) );
	}
}
add\_action( 'um\_custom\_field\_validation\_username\_nickname', 'um\_custom\_validate\_username\_nickname', 30, 3 );

Image - Error message in the form.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6422e877b282ac0ba214c016/file-eSvbv0egbY.png)

### Example with the hook um\_submit\_form\_errors\_hook\_

Code example - Custom validation function attached to the hook.

function um\_custom\_validate\_username\_nickname( $args ) {
	if ( isset( $args\['user\_login'\] ) && isset( $args\['nickname'\] ) && $args\['user\_login'\] == $args\['nickname'\] ) {
		UM()->form()->add\_error( 'user\_login', 'Your username and nickname can not be equal.' );
	}
}
add\_action('um\_submit\_form\_errors\_hook\_', 'um\_custom\_validate\_username\_nickname', 999, 1);

Image - Error message in the form.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6422e877b282ac0ba214c016/file-eSvbv0egbY.png)

Last updated on November 15, 2023

No results found

# Form Builder - Ultimate Member

Form Builder
============

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Overview

Ultimate Member comes with a robust and flexible drag-and-drop form builder used to create the registration, login, and profile forms. With the form builder, you can easily customize the look and layout of the forms and add unlimited fields.

### Form elements

The form builder contains several elements you can use to build your forms. These include master rows, sub rows, columns, field groups, fields, separators, dividers, spacing, and text blocks.

### Master rows

Master rows are the outermost containers of the form, and it is in the master rows that you can add sub rows. The form builder, by default, will contain one master row with one sub row inside of it. It is possible to add, delete and rearrange the order of master rows in the form.

*   **Adding a master row**– To add a new master row to a form, you need to go to the bottom of the form builder and click the plus icon (see below for what to look for). There is no limit to the number of master rows you can add to the form.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/619deee89ccf62287e5f9675/file-VN7VVUhSeu.png)
    

*   **Deleting a master row**– To delete a master row to a form, you need to go to the blue buttons at the top right of the master row and look for the button with a trash can. This is the button that will delete the master row and **all the content** contained within the row. Please note it is not possible to delete the first master row on the form (The delete button will not show for the first master row).  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/619def3864e42a671b639de1/file-WkhFUn7rOc.png)
    

*   **Editing a master row**– To edit a master row on the form, you need to go to the blue buttons at the top right of the master row and click the button with a pencil. Clicking the button will open the edit master row modal, where you can customize the styling and output of the row. This modal makes it very easy to apply custom CSS to this row, and you can also give the row a heading (This is useful if you want to separate your profile into distinct sections).  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/619defa32b380503dfe07de0/file-ezxBxv0q6i.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/619defd89ccf62287e5f9678/file-l4zAuxhhpl.png)
    

This article will provide you step by step instructions on [**How to add fields to a form**](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/188-how-to-add-fields-to-a-form)
.

Last updated on June 27, 2022

No results found

# Setting Passwords in Ultimate Member Registration Forms - Ultimate Member

Setting Passwords in Ultimate Member Registration Forms
=======================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

In Ultimate Member, when setting up registration forms, the presence or absence of a password field can impact the user registration process, especially when using social login or direct subscribe URLs in conjunction with the Social Login or Stripe extensions. Here's how to manage the password field and ensure users can set their password appropriately.

#### Registration Form with Password Field

1.  **Default Behavior:** When a password field exists in the register form, it allows users to add or set a password in your registration form upon registration. This field is essential for users to create an account and log in to your website securely. The `{action_url}` placeholder in the Welcome Email template will direct users to the login page URL. This is because users are expected to have set their password during registration.
2.  **Password Set by Users:** Users are prompted to set their password during registration, ensuring they can log in using their chosen credentials.

#### Registration Form without Password Field

1.  **Placeholder Tags**: If the register form does not include a password field, the `{action_url}` placeholder tag must be used in the Welcome Email template. This allows users to set their password after registration.
2.  **Social Login and Stripe:** In scenarios involving Social Login or Stripe direct subscribe URLs, the `{action_url}` placeholder tag plays a crucial role in allowing users to set their password.
    
    **Note:** This only applies if the Social Login configurations utilize either the **one-step process** or **the 2-step process**. However, it applies only when the password field is not included on the overlay interface. See the sample image below: ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65f16921909e484dba2101fa/file-8M2pk4so4C.png) 
    
3.  **Handling Password Setting:** The absence of a password field can prevent users from setting their password during registration. Therefore, including the `{action_url}` placeholder tag in the Welcome Email template ensures they can set their password after registration.

The presence or absence of a password field in the register form impacts how users set their password. When no password field exists, use the `{action_url}` placeholder tag in the Welcome Email template allows users to set their password after registration, ensuring a seamless registration process.

Last updated on May 7, 2024

No results found

# Field Types - Ultimate Member

Field Types
===========

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Ultimate Member offers a variety of field types. Explore these UM field types in this article and design user-friendly forms that are easy for your users to fill out and complete.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ed81c42d97d1c0459155b/file-4UuwcQbsL4.png)

| Field Types | Description |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ##### Text Box | This field allows you to add a standard single-line text box to your forms where users can enter custom text.  <br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ed9c7c00c2b65208e1d7a/file-QeIwMgvWoS.png) |     |
| ##### Telephone Box | This field allows you to enter numbers in phone number format and save the value in phone number format. You may enter a phone number in its entirety, including the area code, exchange, and extension.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651edb69ed8c6d2f1cffe033/file-riliUwDk6g.png) |     |
| ##### Number | This field allows you to enter numbers; however, letters and most symbols are not permitted. Only numeric values are allowed.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651edcb3c00c2b65208e1d82/file-pzk3rSTziS.png) |
| ##### Textarea | This field allows you to add a multi-line text area to your forms where users can enter custom text. Text area settings will enable you to: specify the height of the text area; limit the number of characters a user can enter into the text area; limit the number of words a user can enter into the text area and turn on/off, allowing HTML to be entered into the text area.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ede9942d97d1c04591569/file-mt913wYJND.png) |
| ##### Dropdown | This field allows you to add a dropdown field to your forms, letting users select a single option from a list of choices. Each choice should be entered once per line in the choices area.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ee1d1ed8c6d2f1cffe03c/file-SfKs4Pie0I.png) |
| ##### Multi-select | This field allows you to add a multi-select field to your forms, letting users select multiple options from a list of choices. You should enter each choice one per line in the choices area. With this field, you can specify the minimum and/or maximum number of selections a user can select when completing this field.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ee307c00c2b65208e1d88/file-vDPDz78kSE.png) |
| ##### Radio | This field allows you to add a radio field to your forms, letting users select one option from a range of options. You should enter each choice one per line in the choices area.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ee3edc00c2b65208e1d8a/file-vjxD5wmbcx.png) |
| **Checkbox** | This field allows you to add a checkbox field to your forms, letting users select multiple options from a range of options. You should enter each choice one per line in the choices area. With this field, you can specify the maximum number of selections a user can select when completing this field.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ee654ed8c6d2f1cffe046/file-upkJEIN1BK.png) |
| **URL** | This field allows you to add a URL field to your forms, letting users enter a website URL. URL specific settings allow you to: decide the link's target (Open in new window or same window); Set the SEO rule for the link (follow or no-follow) and allow you to enter URL alt text that will show on the profile form instead of showing the URL (e.g. [My Website](http://www.mywebsite.com/)<br> instead of [http://www.mywebsite.com](http://www.mywebsite.com)<br>).  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ee7a342d97d1c04591578/file-WxWoxcrGH0.png) |
| **Password** | This field allows you to add a password field to your forms, letting users enter a password. Password specific settings allow you to: specify the minimum and maximum amount of characters the password must have; force a strong password (strong password must contain at least one lowercase, one uppercase, and one number) and allow you to add a confirm password field so that users must enter the password twice in register form only.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ee9ebdfdf44337125d876/file-ToJiZ3dI12.png) |
| **Image upload** | This field allows you to add an image upload field to your forms, letting users upload an image. Image upload specific settings allow you to select which image file types are allowed to be uploaded (e.g., png, jpg, etc.); set a maximum image file size; decide if cropping should be allowed or not and determine whether the cropping ratio is 1:1 or user-defined and customize the text that appears in the image modal before selecting the image.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651eed2242d97d1c0459157e/file-yJrUU3ofVe.png) |
| **File upload** | This field allows you to add a file upload field to your forms, letting users upload a file. File upload specific settings allow you to: select which file types are allowed to be uploaded (e.g., doc, pdf, txt); set a maximum file upload size, and customize the text that appears in the file modal before selecting the file.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ef0c005231a0b51d234e9/file-ECUxFwZyy9.png) |
| **Date picker** | This field allows you to add a date picker field to your forms, letting users select a date. Date picker specific settings will enable you to: select a date range (fixed number of years or specific date range); set the number of years that a user can pick from; select how the date range is applied (equal years before and after the current date, past years only or future years only); choose a date format (e.g., 3 Feb 2015 or Feb 3, 2015); decide on a displayed date format (full date or year only) and disable specific days of the week from being selected.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ef32bc00c2b65208e1d9d/file-SarW3rHucX.png) |
| **Time picker** | This field allows you to add a time picker field to your forms, letting users select a time in the day. Time picker specific settings will enable you to: select a time format (e.g., 12hr or 24hr) and allow you to choose a time interval (default is 60 minutes).<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ef49f05231a0b51d234f0/file-7HDFOH0aHG.png) |
| **Rating** | This field allows you to add a star rating field to your forms, letting users select a star rating. Rating specific settings will enable you to: decide between a five or 10-star rating and set a default rating (leave blank to have no default star rating).  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651ef54dc00c2b65208e1da1/file-3eDTp5yij3.png) |
| **Content Block** | This field allows you to add content to your form, such as custom text. This content cannot be edited by a user but enables you to add information to your forms.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f12166e5cdb01c2a33018/file-ZQzyiespYI.png) |
| **Shortcode** | This field allows you to add a shortcode to your form.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651efaa842d97d1c0459158e/file-NZqS8EcYy0.png) |
| **Spacing** | This field allows you to add spacing to your forms (default spacing is 20px). This field is specifically designed to help you customize the look of your forms.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651efc38ed8c6d2f1cffe070/file-BZaoLsakXC.png) |
| **Divider** | This field allows you to add dividers to your forms. This can be used to separate your forms into distinct sections. Divider specific settings allow you to: set the thickness of the divider (default 4px); select the style (solid, dotted, dashed, double); choose a color for your divider, and add text to the divider.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651efc5105231a0b51d23506/file-UyDWOwNUZ8.png) |
| ##### Google Map | This field allows you to add and display the Google map in the profile form.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651efe0f05231a0b51d23509/file-CGvjLDdYpK.png) |
| ##### YouTube Video | This field allows you to add a YouTube link to display the YouTube video and users can play the video on their profile. It also supports YouTube Shorts links.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651f01c805231a0b51d2350c/file-O2C892lNpi.png) |
| ##### Vimeo Video | This field allows you to add and play the Vimeo video to your profile by adding the Vimeo video link to the field.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651f033b42d97d1c045915a4/file-eaeRO8IQ0o.png) |
| ##### SoundCloud Track | This field allows you to add and play the SoundCloud Track to your profile by adding the SoundCloud link to the field.  <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651f05cfed8c6d2f1cffe081/file-6hTddkkH6o.png) |
| ##### Spotify URL | This field allows you to add the Spotify song, album, or artist's link to display the soundtrack, playlist, or album to your profile and be able to play previews. You can read more about this field type [here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1863-spotify-url-field-type-feature-in-um-2-6-3)<br>.<br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/651f06da05231a0b51d23516/file-R6pjKyvTrm.png) |
| ##### oEmbed | This field type has interactive components for embedding videos, photos, audio, and other content from the [embed providers that are supported](https://developer.wordpress.org/reference/hooks/oembed_providers/)<br>.   <br><br>![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65204d3505231a0b51d23649/file-9lp0gJQIQG.png) ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65204d3fed8c6d2f1cffe1a5/file-ZThXT6imxx.png) |     |

Last updated on November 11, 2023
