# Field Types Setting - Ultimate Member

Field Types Setting
===================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

This article will provide instructions and information about the [field type](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/86-field-types)
 settings. When setting up a new field or editing a field, you can set certain things like field title, privacy, etc. The common settings of a field are the following:

*   **Title** \- This will be the title of your field in the backend for reference. This will not appear on your site.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6228dbe1c1688a6d26a7357e/file-TEsARotxBC.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6228dc0aab585b230a89eb5d/file-xpzjEvS6s1.png)
    
*   **Label** \- This is the label of your field. Text entered in this label settings will appear on your site. If you do not want a label above your field, leave the Label setting field blank.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6228dc65af6f8700f6dad3a5/file-gHH8seHoED.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3bd5c1688a6d26a73d6b/file-EqLSkUj7Eh.png)
    
*   **Meta Key** \- This setting must store info entered in the field in the database. The meta key should be unique. It can only be written in lower case. Use underscore ( \_ ) to separate words. e.g country\_list, job\_title.   
      
    You can't change or edit the meta key of an existing field or when you have selected predefined fields. Meta keys cannot be duplicated. You need to create a new field if you require a new meta key.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3697af6f8700f6dadb45/file-i26ehQ1foF.png)
    
*   **Placeholder**  - The text entered in this setting will be the text that appears within your field on your site.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3c3bab585b230a89f2c5/file-6Vt8n5Wdec.png)
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3c1ec1e53608cf9e6fb4/file-aZ2Haxwagp.png)
    
*   **Help Text** \-  This setting will provide helpful instructions to your users. The text entered in the help text settings will appear when a user hovers on the field tooltip icon.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3c992ce7ed0fb0913ebd/file-dHcim2Xf7h.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3d40c1e53608cf9e6fbb/file-azVwfNzz0V.png)
    
*   **Privacy** \- This setting allows you to select who can view the field on your site. Admin can view all fields regardless of the option set here. You can choose the options below when you click on the dropdown menu:   
    
    *   **Everyone** \- This option makes your field public; everyone can view the field.
    *   **Member** - If this option is selected, only members of your site can view the field.
    *   **Only visible to profile owner and users who can edit other member accounts** - If this option is selected, only the owner and editors can view the field.
    *   **Only visible to profile owner and specific roles -** If this option is selected, the owner and the chosen roles can only view the field.
    *   **Only specific member roles -** If this option is selected, the roles specified in the settings will be the ones who can only view the field.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3d91c1e53608cf9e6fbd/file-oK6ekJSbSk.png)
    
*   **Default  Value** \- This option allows you to prefill a field with a default value before the user enters a value in a field. If you want to have no default value, leave the setting blank.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3deaaf6f8700f6dadb69/file-AQbQYeyPcB.png)
    
*   **Validate -** You can select from this option if you require a field to have a unique validation.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3dde2ce7ed0fb0913ec6/file-0hycXnDjO4.png)
*   **Visibility -** This setting will allow you to select where the field should appear. To know more about the field's visibility, you can read the Fields Visibility Mode documentation [here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/240-fields-visibility-mode)
    .  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3e2bc1e53608cf9e6fc0/file-tKNgT7P9Hq.png)
    
*   **Is this field required? -** Check the box in this setting if you require a user to fill in the field before processing the form.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3e4bc1688a6d26a73d7a/file-aXNDS1y6oT.png)
    
*   **Can user edit this field? -** Check the box in this setting if you want to allow users to edit the field. Admin can edit all fields regardless of the option set here.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3e872ce7ed0fb0913ecd/file-0JTHOCO6QP.png)
    
*   **Icon -** You can choose from 1283 available icons in this setting to display on your site. You can leave this setting blank if you prefer not to display an icon.   
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3ebdaf6f8700f6dadb6a/file-sNkXbEFc2T.png)
    
*   **Add conditional fields support -** You can show or hide the field in this setting using conditional logic based on a specific field's value or condition. To know more about conditional logic, you can read the document [here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/89-conditional-logic)
    .  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a3ef1c1e53608cf9e6fc7/file-JtG8rJRZpQ.png)
    

Some fields may include additional settings, it will be discussed below:

### Text box field settings

*   **Minimum & Maximum length** - You can set the minimum or a maximum number of characters that can be input in the field. To disable these settings, leave it blank or empty.

### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a408f2ce7ed0fb0913ee2/file-8u9XStjnwR.png)Number field settings

*   **Minimum & Maximum number** - You can set the minimum or a maximum number that can be input in the field.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a410cab585b230a89f2e1/file-yNVMzNF6yP.png)

### Textarea  field settings  

*   **Textarea Height** \- You can set the height of the textarea in this setting. The default is 100 pixels.
*   **Maximum length** \- You can set the maximum number of characters input into the field. To disable these settings, leave it blank or empty.
*   **Maximum allowed words -**You can set the maximum number of words that can be input in the field. To disable these settings, leave it blank or empty.
*   **Default Text** \- Set the text default text to be displayed in the field.
*   **Does this textarea accept HTML?** \- You can enable or disable the HTML tags for this field.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a41cdaf6f8700f6dadb78/file-STl2y7Ht35.png)

### Dropdown  field settings

*   **Choices Callback** \- To retrieve choices, you need to add a  callback source in this setting.
*   **Edit Choices** \- You can set the available choices here for the users. You need to enter the options per line. 
*   **Parent option -** You can populate the option depending on the parent option selected. 

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a46bcab585b230a89f2f9/file-oqsdr2suY2.png)

### Multi-select  field settings

*   **Minimum & Maximum number of selections -** Set the minimum or the maximum number of selections a user can pick.
*   **Edit Choices -** You can enter the choices users can select in this setting.
*   **Choices Callback** \- To retrieve choices, you need to add a  callback source in this setting.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a484aaf6f8700f6dadb9c/file-BjjZGeDw58.png)

### Radio  field settings

*   **Edit Choices** \- You can enter the choices users can select in this setting.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a4be5c1e53608cf9e7005/file-A6camsCY6I.png)

### Checkbox  field settings

*   **Maximum number of selections** - You can set the minimum or the maximum number of selections a user can pick.
*   **Edit Choices** \- You can enter the choices that users can select in this setting.

### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a4cc22ce7ed0fb0913f1f/file-ooBhiMVucz.png)URL  field settings

*   **Link Target** \- Choose between opening the link in a new or on the same window.
*   **SEO Follow** -  Choose between follow or no-follow the link by a search engine.
*   **URL Alt Text** \- Change the URL link into a Text link.
![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a4d54ab585b230a89f310/file-5fGCvL8JXb.png)

### Password  field settings

*   **Minimum & Maximum length -** Set the minimum or a maximum number of characters to be inputted in this field.
*   **Force strong password?** \- This setting is only applicable in the register form. If enabled, users will be forced to enter a strong password consisting of at least one lower case letter, one upper case letter, and a number.
*   **Automatically add a confirm password field -** If enabled, a confirmed password field will be added below the password field. This setting only applies in the register form.
*   **Confirm password field label**  \- Set the label above the confirm password field, leave the field empty to use the default label.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622a4f4b2ce7ed0fb0913f2a/file-jGxPRuuEjG.png)

### Image Upload  field settings

*   **Allowed Image Types** -  You can select what image type users can upload in this field. Choose from PNG, JPEG, JPG, & GIF. To select multiple image types, you can press ctrl or command and click on your preferred image types or click and drag the image types. 
*   **Maximum size in bytes** \- You can set the maximum size of the image a user can upload in this field. Leave this setting empty to have unlimited size. 
*   **Crop Feature** - You can enable or disable the crop feature of the image upload and define the ratio. When you click from the drop the menu of the Crop Feature setting, you can select from the following: 
    *   **Turn Off**  \- This is the default setting of the crop feature. It means that the crop feature is disabled.
    *   **Crop and force 1:1 ratio** - When selected, the user is forced to crop the image in a 1:1 ratio.
    *   **Crop and for user-defined ratio** \-The user is forced to crop the image accordingly when selected.
*   **Upload Box Text -** Text entered in this setting will be the headline that will appear in the upload box for this field.
*   **Additional Instructions Text**\- Text entered in this setting will appear below the headline. This is for further instructions.
*   **Upload Box Text (Button) -** Text entered in this setting will appear in the button. e.g., upload  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f0114c1688a6d26a74d93/file-rNiCX4lk7X.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f0343c1688a6d26a74d99/file-1vRcsl1dk6.png)
    

### File Upload Settings

*   **Allowed File Types** \-  You can select the file types your users can upload in this field. You can choose PDF, Text, CSV, DOC, DOCX, ODT, ODS, XLS, XLSX, ZIP, RAR, MP3, JPG, JPEG, PNG, GIF, EPS, PSD, TIF & TIFF. To select multiple file types, you can press Ctrl or Command and click on your preferred file types or click and drag the file types.   
    Note: Use a hook [um\_allowed\_file\_types](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1045-umallowedfiletypes)
     if you need to extend allowed file types.
*   **Maximum size in bytes** \- You can set the maximum size of the file a user can upload in this field. Leave this setting empty to have unlimited size. 
*   **Upload Box Text -** Text entered in this setting will be the headline that will appear in the upload box for this field.
*   **Additional Instructions Text**\- Text entered in this setting will appear below the headline. This is for other instructions.
*   **Upload Box Text (Button)** \- Text entered in this setting will appear in the button. e.g., upload  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f4c36c1e53608cf9e819c/file-hT8xpjQTOn.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f4ebac1e53608cf9e81af/file-AvQB2rNXfA.png)
    

### Date picker  field settings

*   **Set Date Range -** You can set the date range by:
    *   Fixed Number of  Years - If selected, you can set the number of years the user can pick. The default to last is 50 years. ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f51edc1e53608cf9e81f5/file-2CSAsaGTIk.png)
    *   Specific Date Range - If selected, you can manually set the minimum and maximum date/day range.  
        ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f5262c1e53608cf9e81fa/file-HqxtAGhFXw.png) 
*   **Date User-Friendly Format -**  You can select in this option which format of the date you want to display on your site. 
*   **Use custom Date Format -** You can customize the date format in this option. This will [override](https://www.php.net/manual/en/function.date.php)
     the "Date User-Friendly Format"  option. 
*   **Displayed Date Format -** This option allows you to show full dates or show years only.
*   **Disable specific weekdays -** This option allows you to disable particular weekdays from the date picker. 

### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f56a42ce7ed0fb0915064/file-r3lsMjEwLD.png)Time picker  field settings

*   **Time Format -** You can choose the displayed time format for this field.
*   **Default Time -**  Select the default time. You may use all PHP-compatible date formats.
*   **Time Intervals (in minutes) -** Set the minutes interval between each time in the time picker.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f5b95c1688a6d26a74fa6/file-JfDhkLBcQH.png)
    

### Rating  field settings

*   **Rating System -** This option allows you to choose from a 5-stars or 10-stars rating system.
*   **Default Rating -**  You can set the number of stars for a prefilled rating field.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f5d4bc1e53608cf9e826e/file-NqcjPnqemc.png)
    

### Content Block  field settings

*   **Content Editor** - This allows you to edit your content.

### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f5f08ab585b230a8a04d7/file-8d7Phnl537.png)Shortcode  field settings

*   **Enter Shortcode** \- Add the shortcodes in this option to display the shortcodes in the field.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f5fefab585b230a8a04df/file-KtnpFgwGvp.png)
    

### Spacing  field settings

*   **Spacing -** allows you to set the spacing in px on your form. The required spacing is 20px.   
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f60f0af6f8700f6daedec/file-E50PDmL4sz.png)
    

### Divider field settings

*   **Thickness (in pixels)** \- This option allows you to set the thickness or width of the divider in pixels.
*   **Optional Text** \- You can include an optional text with the divider. 
*   **Style -** Select the style of your divider. The options are Solid, dotted, dashed, doubled.
*   **Color -** This allows you to select a color of the divider.
![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/622f61f12ce7ed0fb09150d3/file-XhgV7pv1CF.png)

Last updated on October 28, 2022

### Related Articles

*   [How to Add Fields to a Form](/article/188-how-to-add-fields-to-a-form)

# Fields Visibility - Ultimate Member

Fields Visibility
=================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

You can manage the visibility of fields in a form. Use the field settings **Visibility**, **Privacy** to hide the field for specific modes or specific members. Use [conditional logic](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/89-conditional-logic)
 to hide/show the field depending on the other field value.

### Visibility options

*   **Edit mode only** - field is shown when users are editing profile on the front end. This is only applicable if the user is allowed to edit this field.
*   **View mode only** - field is hidden when users are editing profile. This is the default mode when a user is not allowed to edit the field.
*   **View Everywhere** - field is shown in both modes.

### Privacy options

*   **Everyone** - the field is visible to everyone.
*   **Members** - the field is visible to logged in users.
*   **Only visible to profile owner and users who can edit other member accounts**
*   **Only visible to profile owner and specific roles**\*
*   **Only specific member roles**\*

The setting **Select member roles** appears if the **Only visible to profile owner and specific roles** or **Only specific member roles** option is chosen. In this setting you can choose roles that can view the field.

**Warning:** Fields that may be hidden by Visibility, Privacy or conditional rules must be optional (not required).

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/63413f890f10306a37c0a9f5/file-k9ZPjmTs0F.png)

How to change the visibility of fields
--------------------------------------

To edit the visibility of a field, navigate to the _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit a Form_. Click on the edit button of the field you want to edit.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/57cd74a7c6979108399a01f9/file-VDySKgQGpd.png)

Once the edit button is clicked, a pop-up window will appear and you can now edit or update the Visibility and Privacy of the field.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/57cd739a903360649f6e54a0/file-djzSUC3rHQ.png)

Last updated on November 4, 2023

No results found

# How to Add Fields to a Form - Ultimate Member

How to Add Fields to a Form
===========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

Ultimate Member provides a flexible way to enhance your registration and profile forms by allowing you to add both predefined and custom fields.

*   **Predefined Fields:** These are default fields that are already available in the Ultimate Member system. The platform recognizes them and can be easily included in your forms without any additional setup.
*   **Custom Fields:** These fields are created by you to meet specific requirements that are not covered by the predefined options. You can design custom fields tailored to your users’ needs.

### Important Notes

*   **Profile Screen Limitation:** Ultimate Member does not automatically add any fields to the **wp-admin > Users > Profile** screen. This means any changes made in Ultimate Member forms do not reflect in the standard WordPress user profile area.
*   **Adding Existing Fields:** If you want to include existing fields from the registration form to the profile form, please refer to the article on [Adding Existing Fields from Register Form to Profile Form](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1719-adding-existing-fields-from-register-form-to-profile-form)
     for detailed instructions.

### Steps to Add Fields to a Form

To add fields to a registration or profile form in Ultimate Member, follow these steps:

1.  **Navigate to Forms:**
    *   In your WordPress dashboard, go to **Ultimate Member > Forms**.
    *   Hover over the registration or profile form you want to edit, then click the **Edit** link.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fbed81dd5b956281ae5975/file-h7xunoTh64.png)
2.  **Open the Field Modal:**
    *   Click the **'+'** button in the Form Builder to open the Fields Manager.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc01d544628317ef904824/file-2cIpoGTsOg.png)
3.  **Choose a Field Type:**
    *   In the fields manager, you can either:
        *   **Select a predefined field**: These are fields already available within the system. Once selected, they will automatically appear in your form.  
              
            **Example:**  
            If you select the **"First Name"** field from the **"Predefined Fields,"** it is instantly added to the form builder since it is a recognized, built-in field in Ultimate Member.  
            ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc046a44628317ef904828/file-MU2U5elhSW.png)
        *   **Set up a new field**: This option allows you to create a custom field tailored to your specific needs.  
              
            **Example:**  
            If you choose the **"Number"** field from the **Set Up New Field** section, the **"Add a new field"** window will open, allowing you to configure the field's options as needed.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc1b9415d0b82233064a04/file-FaHjmHwJq8.png) ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc1d8c15d0b82233064a07/file-k3tPFLdF23.png)Once you've finished setting it up, the custom field will be added to the form builder and the **Custom Fields** list in the Fields Manager.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc203be6b2356def90a34e/file-ss9UqpVGoX.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc1edd44628317ef904847/file-coRoFAR3If.png)
4.  **Save the Form:**

*   After adding all your desired fields, click the **Update** button to save your changes.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc22ec44628317ef90484b/file-Wo6cFPg3mB.png)
    

### Common Issues

A common issue users may encounter when adding or creating fields is getting an error message stating: "Your meta key already exists in your fields list." **![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc27b2e6b2356def90a357/file-YR9v2HDmit.png)**

### ****Resolution****

This message appears when you attempt to create a new custom field with a meta key that already exists in the system. To resolve this, you should ensure that the meta key you're trying to use is unique.   **Check in the Fields Manager** (Form Builder > add a field "+" > Custom Field). Hover your cursor over the custom field to see its meta key. If the field and its meta key already exist, consider using the predefined field from the modal or modify your custom field's meta key to make it unique

**![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66fc3268811a2434cb6d461a/file-vY55Rhnx1B.png)**

### **Conclusion**

By utilizing predefined and custom fields, you can tailor the user experience on your site to better meet your community's needs. Make sure to keep track of existing meta keys to avoid duplication errors.

Last updated on October 1, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [Form Builder](/article/85-form-builder)
    
*   [Adding Existing Fields from Register Form to Profile Form](/article/1719-adding-existing-fields-from-register-form-to-profile-form)
    

No results found

# Spotify URL Field Type - Ultimate Member

Spotify URL Field Type
======================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

#### Overview

This article provides information about the added field type  **i****ntroduced in version 2.6.3** which is the **Spotify URL.** 

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6489a8c874c2142995f91d66/file-UGE6149LQ1.png)

This field applies to similar Spotify URL patterns:

*   **Song Link** (e.g.[https://open.spotify.com/track/5YciOakY5dB5dULkiLdCaf?si=26860dc0a5b54654](https://open.spotify.com/track/5YciOakY5dB5dULkiLdCaf?si=26860dc0a5b54654)
    )
*   **Album Link** (e.g.[https://open.spotify.com/album/4mMan8IGNJUhZ6du15ki5T?si=rOYtN4mjSQ-utBOlFOFaZw](https://open.spotify.com/album/4mMan8IGNJUhZ6du15ki5T?si=rOYtN4mjSQ-utBOlFOFaZw)
    )
*   **Artist Link** (e.g. [https://open.spotify.com/artist/2DaxqgrOhkeH0fpeiQq2f4?si=JFwzsIi6S06Q2qKNlqvqNA](https://open.spotify.com/artist/2DaxqgrOhkeH0fpeiQq2f4?si=JFwzsIi6S06Q2qKNlqvqNA)
    )

The user will be able to view and edit the Spotify field in **User's profile > Edit Profile** once you have added the Spotify URL field and updated the profile form in **wp-admin>Ultimate Member> Forms>Edit or Add Profile Form.** This [article](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/188-how-to-add-fields-to-a-form)
 will teach you how to add a field to a form. 

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6489b37d1211660f0912da66/file-fSy1f3bG9I.png)

**Note: The Spotify Field will only appear in the user's profile once they have added a link and updated their profile.**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6489b519885de842a5d8c13f/file-T88GM9EblS.png)

#### Example 1 - Song Link

The user will be able to see the song's iframe and play a preview of the song on their profile page after adding a song link in the Spotify URL field and updating their profile.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6489b5ed5fc2fe6b6dc5fea0/file-2BoRybqX1v.png)

#### Example 2 - Album Link

The user will be able to see the album's iframe and play a preview of the songs listed in the album on their profile page after adding an album link in the Spotify URL field and updating their profile.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6489b82b7f8c2575e3544ddf/file-CGONMuyEF4.png)

#### Example 3 - Artist Link

The user will see the artist's iframe, which has a list of the artist's tracks. They may listen to a preview of the artist's songs on their profile page after adding the artist's link in the Spotify URL field and updating their profile.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6489b9c674c2142995f91d7b/file-ZObf7JPGem.png)

Last updated on June 15, 2023

No results found

# How to use User Roles - Ultimate Member

How to use User Roles
=====================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

WordPress uses "user roles" to manage such things as users access, privacy, privileges, etc. Ultimate Member extends this functionality to limit profile access.

Please read the article [User Roles Settings](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/103-user-roles-settings)
 first to get to know about role settings.

**Contents:**

*   [How to Add, Edit or Remove user role](#add_role)
    
*   [Assign user role](#assign_role)
      
    *   [A role for a new user](#default_role)
        
    *   [Change users role manually](#change_role_manually)
        
    *   [Change user role on purchase](#change_role_auto)
        
*   [Configure role fields](#fields)
    
*   [Add role-specific content](#role-specific_content)
    *   [Shortcode restriction](#shortcode_restriction)
        
    *   [Gutenberg Blocks restriction](#blocks_restriction)
        
*   [Make profile role-specific](#role-specific-profile)
    
*   [Members Directory role settings](#members_directory)
    
*   [Limit profile access by role](#profile_access)
    *   [User role settings](#role_settings)
        
    *   [Role Priority](#role_priority)
        
*   [Grant access to the admin dashboard to a custom role](#admin_access)
    

How to Add, Edit or Remove user role [#](#add_role)

----------------------------------------------------

Click the **Add New** button on the top of the page _\[Ultimate Member > User Roles\]_ to add a new user role. You'll see a form **Add New Role**. Configure role settings, then click **Create Role**.

Click the **Edit** link under the user role title to edit it. You'll see a form **Edit Role**. Change role settings you need, then click **Update Role**.

Select the role and use the bulk action **Delete** to remove user roles created via Ultimate member. This bulk action doesn’t remove user roles created via third-party plugins.

Image - Add or edit user role.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/617aed422b380503dfdff5d2/file-zCZKJr2yXE.png)

Image - Delete UM Custom Role.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6230601b2ce7ed0fb0915682/file-gu1RpuWuzq.png)

Assign user role [#](#assign_role)

-----------------------------------

### A role for a new user [#](#default_role)

Each user has one or several roles. WordPress assigns a role when creating a user. There are several settings that may influence the user role assigned to a new user. Part of these settings work in the registration form only, and part of them works in the checkout form only.

Registration flow:

1.  The setting **"New User Default Role"** on _wp-admin > Settings > General_ is a native WordPress setting for new users. This setting is used if there are no other settings.
2.  The setting **"Registration Default Role"** on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Appearance > Registration Form_ is a common setting for all UM registration forms. It has a higher priority than the setting "New User Default Role" but works only in UM registration forms.
3.  The setting **"Assign role to form"** on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit Form > Customize this form_ is a setting for a certain registration form. It has higher priority than settings "New User Default Role" and "Registration Default Role" but works for a certain UM registration form.  
    **Note:** The site can have multiple registration forms.

Checkout flow (for WooCommerce only):

1.  WooCommerce always assigns the role "Customer" on checkout.
2.  The setting **"Assign this role to users when an order is completed/processing"** on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > WooCommerce_ may be used to assign an additional user role on checkout. [Details](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/131-woocommerce-setup#settings_extension)
      
    **Note:** Users in WordPress can have multiple roles.
3.  The setting **"When this product is bought move user to this role"** on the page _wp-admin > Products > All Products > Edit Product_ has a higher priority than the setting "Assign this role to users when an order is completed/processing" but works for a certain product. [Details](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/131-woocommerce-setup#product_settings)
    

### Change users role manually [#](#change_role_manually)

Administrators can use the bulk action **"Change role to..."** on _wp-admin > Users > All Users_ to change the user role manually.

Image - How to set the user role manually.

![WP-Users-Change-role.png](https://d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/inline/27673/11bf8a21bd97e880936c86c616e945a9dce19d5d/064edd49e3ec76c9ed4a298fad563c759867d284/WP-Users-Change-role.png)

### Change user role on purchase [#](#change_role_auto)

Our extension "Ultimate Member - WooCommerce" can change or add a user role automatically when the user purchases a product or subscription.

Read the article [WooCommerce Setup](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/131-woocommerce-setup)
 to get know how to configure this extension.

Configure role fields [#](#fields)

-----------------------------------

You can use field types **"Roles (Dropdown)"** and **"Roles (Radio)"** to set the user role in the registration form or change it in the profile form. But not all roles are available. Role fields may contain user roles created by Ultimate Member and predefined role Subscriber. User roles created by third-party plugins are excluded to avoid conflicts. Predefined roles with high access levels (such as Administrator or Editor) are excluded to avoid security vulnerability.

You have to create a role using the button **"Add New"** on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles_, then add this role to the list of "Edit Choices" in the field settings.

Image - Configure role field choices.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/607550cf4466ce6ddc5f3e22/file-g8VPXvNY7P.png)

Add role-specific content [#](#role-specific_content)

------------------------------------------------------

### Shortcode restriction [#](#shortcode_restriction)

You can show or hide special content to the specific user roles. Wrap the content with the shortcode `[um_show_content]`.

See also [Restrict content on a page/post to specific users community role](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/226-restrict-content-on-a-page-post-to-specific-users-community-role)
.

**Example:** Show content to specific roles.

\[um\_show\_content roles="member"\] <!-- insert content here --> \[/um\_show\_content\]

**Example:** Hide content from specific roles.

\[um\_show\_content not="member"\] <!-- insert content here --> \[/um\_show\_content\]

You can add multiple target roles, just use ',' e.g. `[um_show_content roles="member,candidates,pets"]`

### Gutenberg Blocks restriction [#](#blocks_restriction)

You can show or hide a Gutenberg Block to the specific user roles. Use settings in the box **"Ultimate Member: Content Restriction"** for the block.

Turn on the **Enable the "Content Restriction" settings for the Gutenberg Blocks** setting on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Access > Content Restriction_ to enable this feature if disabled.

See also [Restrict Gutenberg Blocks](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1624-restrict-gutenberg-blocks)
.

**Example:** Show a block to specific roles.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65463b704cab6a2efab4e339/file-y4SlssezK1.png)

Make profile role-specific [#](#role-specific-profile)

-------------------------------------------------------

**Note:** This feature should be used if you need several different profile forms. Don't use this feature if you need a single common profile form, as is the default.

You can customize profiles for user roles. Create multiple role-specific profile forms with different sets of fields and settings and add them to the **User** page. The plugin will display profile form depending on the profile owner user role and profile form setting **"Make this profile form role-specific"**.

Use the setting **"Make this profile form role-specific"** in the box **"Customize this form"** to make a profile role-specific. This setting is a multi-select. Select user roles whose profiles should use this profile form. Plugin displays profile form for all users if this setting is blank.

See also [Unique profiles for different user roles](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/100-unique-profiles-for-different-user-roles)
.

**Example:** Use the first profile form for the subscriber's profile and use the second profile form for other profiles.

Image - Make profile form role-specific for a single role.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/60e41a3405ff892e6bc2de9e/file-1UAMQBXHnj.png)

Image - Make profile form role-specific for multiple roles.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/60e41a3e8556b07a2884c95e/file-FvmLz3IBiU.png)

Image - User page with two role-specific profile forms.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/60e41a448556b07a2884c95f/file-CUA8bkDgtO.png)

Members Directory role settings [#](#members_directory)

--------------------------------------------------------

You can create several member directories with different settings. Each member directory has a unique shortcode that you can paste to the page to display. You can get the shortcode, change settings or create a new member directory on the page _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Member Directories_.

Use the setting **"User Roles to Display"** to select what user roles this member directory should display. See [Member Directories 2.1.0+](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1513-member-directories-2-1-0)
.

Image - Limit members' directory by roles.

![UM-Member-Directories-Edit--General-Options--User-Roles-to-Display.png](https://d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/inline/27673/f20137f874a3dfe34487965b966cffd758f2e687/b7cd4f653bcc150e84c3d95ca6e8a2c47e501e81/UM-Member-Directories-Edit--General-Options--User-Roles-to-Display.png)

Limit profile access by role [#](#profile_access)

--------------------------------------------------

### User role settings [#](#role_settings)

Use user role settings **"Can view other member profiles?"** and **"Can view these user roles only"** on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles > Edit Role_ to manage profiles visibility. Turn on the **"Can view other member profiles?"** setting to allow members with this user role to view other members profiles. Leave the **"Can view these user roles only"** setting blank to allow members with this user role to view any profiles.

Image - Limit profile access by roles.

![UM-User-Roles-Edit-Role--Profile-Access.png](https://d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/inline/27673/3aed8c74c1ae1bd5ee707187148944fda4c3176a/331472b8aa5b453c2c22c74b0104447ef3a6cfe4/UM-User-Roles-Edit-Role--Profile-Access.png)

### Role Priority [#](#role_priority)

WordPress users may have several roles. Ultimate Member uses settings from the role with the highest priority. Read the article [How to set Role Priority for user roles](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1494-how-to-set-role-priority-for-user-roles)
 for details.

Image - Role Priority.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6059cb680ad05379b10cfa14/file-Z8hMMaUHRe.png)

Grant access to the admin dashboard to a custom role [#](#admin_access)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

Turn on the role setting **"Can access wp-admin"** to allow access to the admin dashboard for specific roles.

The role setting **"Force hiding admin bar in frontend"** can be used to hide the admin bar for specific roles. It's better to turn off this setting for roles which can access _wp-admin_.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/63a04c28f439e8768b4f6422/file-P1iuu2MTyK.png)

WordPress uses capabilities to limit access to the admin dashboard. Verify that your custom admin role has these capabilities:

*   **read** - required to access the site.
*   **edit\_posts** - required to access _wp-admin_.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/63a04c7ef439e8768b4f6423/file-LUmF1DF1h7.png)

Users may need additional capabilities to access specific screens of the admin dashboard. See [User Roles Settings](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/103-user-roles-settings#capabilities)
.

Last updated on January 27, 2025

### Related Articles

*   [User Roles Settings](/article/103-user-roles-settings)
    
*   [How to set Role Priority for user roles](/article/1494-how-to-set-role-priority-for-user-roles)
    
*   [Hiding/Showing admin bar for specific user roles](/article/243-hiding-showing-admin-bar-for-specific-user-roles)
    
*   [How to set up a user role that can edit other member without wp-admin access](/article/1872-how-to-set-up-a-user-role-that-can-edit-other-member-without-wp-admin-access)
    

No results found

# Block Disposable Email Domains - Ultimate Member

Block Disposable Email Domains
==============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Overview

Disposable Email Domains are temporary in the sense that they may expire or self-destruct.  This plugin will help you update your Block Email Address option to detect spam or disposable email domains in the Ultimate Member's Register Forms, allowing you to prevent Spam Registration. For the installation and setup guide, go [here.](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1889-installation)

### Block Disposable Email Domains

Once activated, this will update the list of blocked email domains in **Ultimate Member > Settings > Access > Other > Blocked Disposable Email Domains**. The extension updates the list daily if there's a new disposable email domain to block. This merges with the Blocked Emails option, so you can add your Email address and Domains using the existing option. 

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64ac200d90a5ec74a42ff221/file-eFrV1RzJZS.png)

Last updated on November 9, 2023

No results found

# Cover Photo Uploader in Register Form - Ultimate Member

Cover Photo Uploader in Register Form
=====================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Overview

This plugin empowers users to personalize their profiles from the outset by enabling cover photo uploads directly within the Register Form. Activating this extension enhances the user registration experience, providing a seamless way for individuals to add a distinctive cover photo during the initial sign-up process. Elevate user engagement and profile customization on your platform with the Add Cover Photo to Register Form plugin.

### Setting Up Cover Photo Upload in Register Form

Ensure the plugin is activated, then follow these steps to integrate cover photo upload functionality in the Register Form:

1.  Go to wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit Registration Form.
2.  Click the "+" sign button in the Form Builder to add a new field.
3.  In the Field Manager, select **"Cover Photo"** under the predefined fields.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f6cf2687c016dc15b762a/file-He7Ixv5Rsl.png)
    
4.  After adding the "Cover Photo" field, click **"Update"** to save changes.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f6d7b6e5cdb01c2a33022/file-E3cgjcpIn4.png)
    
5.  Visit your site's registration form to verify the cover photo uploader functionality.
    *   Users can select a photo from their folder by clicking the **"Upload"** button.  
        ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f81cc687c016dc15b762e/file-v40eNBRbLf.png)
    *   The selected photo can be cropped.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6569e5e834a236193adc8df5/file-EZaHRliBhk.png)
    *   Users have the option to change the photo before finalizing registration.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6569e605f0a5f10af23cfc97/file-Vkns3mMHyV.png)
6.  Upon successful registration, the uploaded cover photo is automatically displayed when the user views or visits their profile.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6569e7feb9c5ac4b9b74c5eb/file-47nmFFYJsN.png)
    

Last updated on December 1, 2023

No results found

# Displaying Member Directories - Ultimate Member

Displaying Member Directories
=============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Overview

By default, Ultimate Member installs a members directory on your site on a page called members. You need to enable the default feature in **wp-admin>****Ultimate Member> Settings> General> Users> Enable Members Directory.  
**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/633ae15f3f38fe1e468631be/file-iKgRWECg6I.png)

If you want to create another Member Directory on a new page and display it on your site, you need to do the following steps below:

### 1\. Adding a New Member Directory

Go to wp-admin>Ultimate Member> Member Directories, then click on the **Add New** button.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6239cfe1c1e53608cf9eb475/file-JScvheGWvG.png)

Please type the name of your new member directory and set it according to your preference. To learn more about the settings of Member Directories, go to this [article](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1513-member-directories-2-1-0)
. Once you're done with the settings, click on the create button to save and copy the shortcode of your new member directory.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6239d1c7af6f8700f6db1f40/file-F9qozbl2mD.png)

### 2\. Creating a New Page

As mentioned above, Ultimate Member automatically creates a page for the member directory named **Members**. You can create multiple member directories on the member page by adding the shortcodes to the page. However, if you want to create a separate page for your new member directory, go to wp-admin>Pages. Click on the **Add New** button to create a new page.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6239d44ac1e53608cf9eb4b6/file-eAyiCQ3sdv.png)Set the name of your new page and select the shortcode block to **paste the shortcode** of your new Member Directory. You can set the visibility or put restrictions on your page and make sure to check the settings before publishing it. 

### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6239d4f92ce7ed0fb0918347/file-f6Wuaibzvn.png)  
3\. Displaying the Member Directory 

When your page has been published, it will be added to the Pages. To display your member directory on your site menu, go to wp-admin>Appearance> Menus. Select the menu where you want to add the new member directory. Under the **Add menu items,** go to the **Pages** section and select your new page by clicking on the check box and clicking the **Add to Menu** button to add the page in the **menu structure**.

You can organize the items by dragging them into the order you prefer. Set who can see the menu on your site by clicking on the dropdown. Once you're done with the settings, click on the **Save Menu** button to save the changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6239d9a42ce7ed0fb091837a/file-z5huJbUhia.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6239d921c1688a6d26a7811b/file-VprFtAOSMF.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6239d849c1e53608cf9eb4ea/file-zx42AAafLC.png)

Go to your site and check if you have successfully displayed your new member directory.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6239da66af6f8700f6db1f98/file-2nAEbENWGR.png)

Last updated on October 3, 2022

No results found

# Member Directories 2.1.0+ - Ultimate Member

Member Directories 2.1.0+
=========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

This article describes member directory settings. You can use the existing member directory or create a new one on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Member Directories_.

**Contents**

*   [General Options](#general)
    
*   [Sorting Options](#sorting)
    
*   [Profile Card](#card)
    
*   [Search Options](#search)
    
*   [Results & Pagination](#results)
    

### General Options [#](#general)

**View type(s)** \- this option allows you to select view types of the Member Directory. There are able Grid and List views. If you select more than 1 view type, there will be displayed the change layout button at the member directory front-end.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66196ce25b008e1c56a993f4/file-gqSp0AXHTx.png)

**Default view type** - this option allows you to select the default view type from already selected view type(s).

**User roles to display** – this option allows you to select which user roles are displayed in the directory. If left blank the directory will display the profile cards of all members of your site.

**Only show members who have uploaded a profile photo** – this option allows you to only show members who have uploaded a profile photo. If turned on any member who does not upload a profile photo will not be shown in the directory. This option is turned off by default.

**Only show members who have uploaded a cover photo** – this option allows you to only show members who have uploaded a cover photo. If turned on any member who does not upload a cover photo will not be shown in the directory. This option is turned off by default.

**Only show specific users (Enter one username per line)** - this option allows you to show only selected members by username.

**Exclude specific users (Enter one username per line)** - this option allows you to exclude only selected members by username.

**Only show members who have completed their profile** - this option allows you to show only members who completed their profile with the **Required completeness (%)** option value. The last one is visible when the "Only show members who have completed their profile" option is enabled. These options are integrated by the [Profile Completeness](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/profile-completeness/)
 extension.

**Only show members who have verified their profile** - this option allows you to only show members who verified their profile. This option is integrated by the [Verified Users](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/verified-users/)
 extension.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66196d75477c990280e4380c/file-WRN1I84565.png)

### Sorting Options [#](#sorting)

**Default sort users by** - this option allows you to decide the order of how members are shown in the directory.

You can sort members by:

| Plugin | Fields |
| --- | --- |
| Ultimate Member | New users first. Is used by default |
| Old users first |
| Display name |
| First name |
| Last login |
| Last name |
| Other (Custom Field). Allows you to insert field key to the option "Meta key" and "Label of custom sort" below |
| Random |
| Username |
| Nickname |
| Ultimate Member - Followers | Most followed |
| Least followed |
| Ultimate Member - Friends | Most friends |
| Least friends |
| Ultimate Member - myCRED | Most points |
| Least points |
| Ultimate Member - Profile Completeness | Most completed |
| Least completed |
| Ultimate Member - User Reviews | Highest rated first |
| Lowest rated first |
| Ultimate Member - Verified Users | Verified accounts first |
| Not verified accounts first |

**Enable custom sorting** - this option allows you to show the dropdown with sorting options, which are selected in the "Choose fields to enable in sorting" option.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66196f1bdac5c26585fc645a/file-w8Y9jrPgQJ.png)

**Choose fields to enable in sorting** - this option allows you to select the fields for the member directory sorting. If you select the "Other (Custom Field)." option, please fill in the fields "Meta Key" and "Label" below.

**Note**: The value of the "Default sort users by" option will be added to the list on the front end if it is not selected here. If the total number of unique sorting options for a directory is 1 or less, the sorting field will be hidden on the front end. And users will be sorted by default sort value.

### Profile Card [#](#card)

| Setting | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Enable Profile Photo | This option allows you to show or hide the user profile photos on the member directory. |
| Enable Cover Photo | This option allows you to show or hide the user's cover photos on the member directory.  _Note: There isn’t a cover photo in the "List" layout._ |
| Show Display Name | This option allows you to show or hide the user display name on the member directory. |
| Hide Followers Stats | Integrated by the Followers extension. If unchecked, get from Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > Followers > Show followers stats in the member directory. |
| Hide Follow Button | Integrated by the Followers extension. If unchecked, get from Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > Followers > Show follow button in the member directory. |
| Hide Rating Stats | Integrated by the User Reviews extension. If unchecked, get from Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > Reviews > Show user rating in the members directory. |
| Hide myCRED Badges | Integrated by the myCRED extension. If unchecked, gets from Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > myCRED > Show user badges in Member Directories? |
| Hide Friends Stats | Integrated by the Friends extension. If unchecked, get from Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > Friends > Show friends stats in the member directory. |
| Hide Friend Button | Integrated by the Friends extension. If unchecked, get from Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > Friends > Show friend button in the member directory. |
| Hide Online Stats | Integrated by the Online extension. If unchecked, get from Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > Online > Show online stats in the member directory. |
| Hide Message Button in Directory? | Integrated by the Private Messages extension. If unchecked, get from Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > Private Messaging > Show messages button in the member directory. |
| Show Tagline Below Profile Name | This option allows you to show the user meta below the user display name. This could be an important piece of information relevant to your site e.g. you could show the user’s nationality below the display name. Below this option is the user meta "Choose field(s) to display in tagline" |
| Show Extra User Information Below Tagline? | This option allows you to show extra user meta below the tagline. If you select yes it will show the option "Enable reveal section transition by default" Show the information in a reveal section (the information is hidden until the user clicks the arrow) or show it on page load. Below this option is the user meta selector "Choose field(s) to display in reveal section" where you can choose which field(s) to add to this area of the profile card. |
| Show Social Connect Icons | This option allows you to social icons on the profile card which link to a user’s social media accounts. The social icons are LinkedIn to the social fields which are found in the pre-defined fields list. If a user does not enter any information in the relevant social media field then the social icon will not show e.g if a user does not enter their Twitter username in the Twitter field then the Twitter field will not show on their profile card. |

**Enable Search feature** - This option allows you to show or hide the general search line on the member directory. If you enable the search "User Roles that can use search" option will appear below.

Ultimate Member uses for searching all Ultimate Member custom metadata, created via _Ultimate Member > Forms > Fields_ screen and WP native fields like `username`, `user_email`, `display_name`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `user_nicename`.

**User Roles that can use search** - This option allows you to select which user roles can use the general search line. If left blank anyone can use the search line.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66197751d9cffa6c1049d4cb/file-dUGoODNsBX.png)

**Exclude fields from search** \- This option allows you to choose specific fields to exclude from the search function, including all other fields in the search. This can help refine search results by excluding certain information from the search results.

_Example:_ If you select "Email" and "Phone Number" in the "Exclude fields from search" setting, the search function will exclude matches in the "Email" and "Phone Number" fields from the search results, including all other fields in the search.

_Scenario:_ Imagine you have a database of customer records with fields for "First Name," "Last Name," "Email," and "Phone Number." If you select "Email" and "Phone Number" in the "Exclude fields from search" setting, any search queries will only consider the "First Name" and "Last Name" fields.

_Outcome:_ So, if a user searches for "John" and "John" is in the "First Name" field for a user, that user will appear in the search results. However, if "John" is in the "Email" or "Phone Number" fields, that user will not appear in the search results, even though their name matches the search query.

**Fields to search by** - This option allows you to choose specific fields to include in the search function, excluding all other fields from the search. This can help refine search results to only include relevant information.

_Example:_ If you select "First Name" and "Last Name" in the "Fields to search by" setting, the search function will only look for matches in the "First Name" and "Last Name" fields, excluding all other fields from the search.

_Scenario:_ Imagine you have a database of customer records with fields for "First Name," "Last Name," "Email," and "Phone Number." If you select "First Name" and "Last Name" in the "Fields to search by" setting, the search function will only consider these two fields for matching search queries.

_Outcome:_ So, if a user searches for "John Doe," the search function will only look for matches in the "First Name" and "Last Name" fields. If there is a customer named "John Doe" in your database, they will appear in the search results. However, if "John Doe" is in the "Email" or "Phone Number" fields but not in the "First Name" or "Last Name" fields, that customer will not appear in the search results because the search is only considering the "First Name" and "Last Name" fields for matches.

**Enable Filters feature** - This option allows you to show or hide the filters on the member directory. If you enable the filters "User Roles that can use filters" and "Choose filter(s) meta to enable" options will appear below.

**User Roles that can use filters** - This option allows you to select which user roles can use the filters. If left blank anyone can use the filters.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66198fd8dac5c26585fc6480/file-Zzz1lbBfJ3.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66198645dac5c26585fc646f/file-FKTegyd3yI.png)**Choose filter(s) meta to enable** - This option allows you to select fields that will be shown in the filters section. The filters section can be expanded by default via the **Expand the filter bar by default** option below. Please note that not all field types are allowed in the search filters, so not all of your custom fields will appear in this list.

There are 4 filter types by default in Ultimate Member core and extensions: Dropdown, Slider range, Date range, and Time range.

The values available in the filters (Dropdown options, Slider range limits, Date range period) depend on the members' data. Therefore, a dropdown option is shown if there are members who have selected this option in their profiles. The option is hidden if no one has selected this option in their profile.

Table - Supported filter fields and their types.

| Field | Type |
| --- | --- |
| _Ultimate Member Core predefined fields_ |     |
| Country | Dropdown |
| Gender | Dropdown |
| Languages | Dropdown |
| Roles\* | Dropdown |
| Age(Birth Date field) | Slider range |
| Last Login | Date range |
| User Registered | Date range |
| _Ultimate Member Core custom fields_ |     |
| Timepickers | Time range |
| Datepickers | Date range |
| Rating fields | Slider range |
| Checkboxes | Dropdown |
| Radio buttons | Dropdown |
| Selects/Multiselects | Dropdown |
| _UM: myCRED_ |     |
| Rank | Dropdown |
| Balance | Slider range |
| _UM: Online_ |     |
| Online Status | Dropdown |
| _UM: Profile Completeness_ |     |
| Completeness | Slider range |
| _UM: User Reviews_ |     |
| User Rating | Slider range |
| _UM: Woocommerce_ |     |
| Billing Country | Dropdown |
| Shipping Country | Dropdown |
| Total Orders | Slider range |
| Total Spent | Slider range |
| _UM: User Tags_ |     |
| User Tags custom field | Dropdown |

**Note:** The **Role** is a reserved word to determine if the field is a role field.

Image - Filters in the member directory

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/66198ee3ef6cd1080a2eae48/file-4jB7mZxsgD.png)

**Admin filtering** - this option allows you to select the predefined filters that display users on the page's first loading. All fields available for front-end filtering are available here. Particular attention should be paid to the fact that if a field for a predefined filter is selected, and then filtered by it using the “Choose filter(s) meta to enable” fields, then the “Choose filter (s) meta to enable” fields will be more priority.

### Results & Pagination [#](#results)

**Show results only after search/filtration** - you can decide how to display members in the directory. On page loading or after the searching/filtration.

**Maximum number of profiles, Number of profiles per page, Number of profiles per page (for Mobiles & Tablets)** \- you can decide the number of profiles to show on each page (Please note: the higher the number the longer the page will take to load) and there is also an option to limit the total number of profiles that will be shown in this directory (leave blank to show all users in the directory).

**Results Text** - This option allows you to customize the text that is shown after a user performs a search. The tag `{total_users}` will show the number of users that match the search criteria. The same but for single result **Single Result Text** option.

**Custom text if no users were found** - This option allows you to customize the text that is shown after a user performs a search and the search returns empty (no users were matched the search criteria).

Last updated on April 15, 2024

No results found

# How to set Role Priority for user roles - Ultimate Member

How to set Role Priority for user roles
=======================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Role Priority

Users may have multiple roles. In this case, the Ultimate Member plugin uses settings from the role with the highest priority. Settings from the first role are used if roles have equal priority. Role priority is a number and a bigger number gives a higher priority. The default user role priority is 0.

Recommended priorities for roles:

*   Participant (bbp\_participant) => 5
*   Moderator (bbp\_moderator) => 10
*   Keymaster (bbp\_keymaster) => 15
*   Customer (customer) => 30
*   Subscriber (subscriber) => 35
*   **Your custom roles => 40**
*   Contributor (contributor) => 50
*   Author (author) => 55
*   Shop manager (shop\_manager) => 70
*   Editor (editor) => 75
*   Administrator (administrator) => 90

You can set user **Role Priority** in the "Publish" box on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles > Edit Role._

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65cd71831359983245253bea/file-25iJOtcnla.png)

**Example:**

When you have 2 user roles "Subscriber" (subscriber) and "Participant" (bbp\_participant), one should be the primary role. If we want the "Subscriber" to be used as the primary user role, we can add the higher priority number to it. The primary user role makes the login and register process follow its role settings. It also influences profile access and permissions to use various accounts, member directories, and profile features.

Last updated on February 15, 2024

*   [User Roles Settings](/article/103-user-roles-settings)

# Displays Country flag in Member Directory and User Profiles - Ultimate Member

Displays Country flag in Member Directory and User Profiles
===========================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

This plugin provides a straightforward solution to enhance user profiles and the member directory by incorporating the display of country flags. Once activated, users' respective country flags become visible in both the member directory and individual user profiles. This simple yet effective feature adds a personalized touch to user information, fostering a more engaging and visually enriched community experience. Elevate the representation of user locations on your platform with the Country Flag Display plugin.

### Setting Up the Field

1.  Navigate to wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > **Edit Profile Form**.
2.  Click the **"+"** sign button in the form builder to access the fields manager.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f622d687c016dc15b7625/file-fzxQGxCd2m.png)
    
3.  Select the pre-defined field **"Country."![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f65128fd0677319115f28/file-ae3r13qdj2.png)**
4.  Click **"Update"** to save the changes.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f65b92d28585006d01783/file-A5g0OlSbbE.png)
    

### Member Directory Settings

1.  Go to wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Member Directories > Edit a Member Directory > **Profile Card**.
2.  In the Profile Card section, find the **"Show extra user information below tagline?"** and enable it to display additional settings.
3.  In **"Choose field(s) to display in extra user information",** click the **"Add New Custom Field"** button and select **"Country"** from the drop-down menu.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f67ef6e5cdb01c2a3301c/file-uXSE5tUNqN.png)
4.  Click **"Update"** to save changes.

### Country Flag Display

The country flag will be visible in both the user's profile and the member directory once the user has selected their country. This plugin offers a simple yet impactful way to showcase user locations within your community.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f6afe00e96206bf001005/file-VG17rypa7C.png)

Last updated on December 1, 2023

# Creating Metatable for Member Directories - Ultimate Member

Creating Metatable for Member Directories
=========================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Ultimate Member, starting from version 2.1.3, offers the ability to create and utilize a custom database table, enhancing the performance of member directories. If you're encountering speed issues with your member directory or are using multiple filters that could potentially slow down your website, we recommend enabling this feature.

The new database table contains essential user data for searching, filtering, and sorting, significantly improving member directory performance, which is particularly beneficial for large and heavily loaded databases.

To create the custom table, follow these steps:

1.  **Backup:** Create a full backup of your website and database through your hosting provider's control panel.
2.  **Enable Feature:** Go to wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Advanced > Features, then check the "Enable custom table for usermeta" checkbox.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65f4584d54e485464f3dcca1/file-tPW2PjqPkz.png)
    
3.  **Run Update:** Click the **"Run"** button and wait for the upgrade process to complete.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65f45cc154e485464f3dcca8/file-WQ5qwZrJGt.png)
    

This process updates the database table `{$table_prefix}um_metadata`, which was created when the plugin was activated, and copies all user data into this table. The data in this custom table is a duplicate of the data stored in the default database tables `{$table_prefix}users` and `{$table_prefix}usermeta`. The plugin automatically updates the data in this custom table whenever the data in the default tables is updated.

If you notice any issues with searching and filtering in the member directory, you can manually update this custom table. Simply turn off the feature, save the changes, then turn the feature back on and run the update process again.

Last updated on March 15, 2024

No results found

# Enable Profile Photo Uploader in Registration Forms - Ultimate Member

Enable Profile Photo Uploader in Registration Forms
===================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**Note:** This feature was previously available for both the **Account** and **Registration** forms. However, starting with **version 2.9.0**, uploading a profile or cover photo is now only possible during the **registration process**. Uploading photos from the **Account** page is **no longer supported** due to security improvements in the file/image upload system.

* * *

### Overview

This plugin provides seamless integration for users to upload their profile photos directly within the **Registration Form**. With an easy setup, this feature enhances the registration process by allowing users to personalize their profiles right from the start. Upon installation and activation, users can conveniently upload their profile pictures, contributing to a more engaging and visually appealing experience. For detailed instructions on installing the plugin, please refer to the [Basic](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1889-installation)
 [Extensions Installation Guide](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1889-installation)
.

* * *

### Security Update (Version 2.9.0)

A security update introduced in version 2.9.0 improves the safety of file and image uploads. As a result, the ability to upload or change profile and cover photos has been affected:

*   **Account Page:** Due to security improvements in the file/image upload system, uploading a profile or cover photo is no longer supported from the Account page.
*   **Registration Page:** Users can still upload or change their profile photo during the **registration process**.

* * *

### Setting Up Profile Photo Upload in the Registration Form

Follow these steps to integrate the profile photo upload functionality into the Registration Form after activating the plugin:

1.  Navigate to `wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit Registration Form`.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/674daae2b4a7aa04e069a3fa/file-vmSuavqAVe.png)
    
2.  Click the **"+"** button to add a new field in the **UM Form Builder**.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/674dab47830fb1171cb89412/file-Y6koNa1ORy.png)
    
3.  In the **Fields Manager** window, select **"Profile Photo"** from the predefined fields.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/674daba14f36c9127dc0c35a/file-WSULKy8bck.png)
    
4.  After adding the **"Profile Photo"** field, click the **"Update"** button to save the changes.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/674dac14cde58670ddf6fbcd/file-zpoBvPUQsS.png)
    

With this setup, users will be able to upload their profile photos seamlessly during registration, creating a more personalized and visually enriched profile.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/674dae869a0ff05dcf34bfd7/file-F89aqp4qNF.png)

* * *

**Note:** If you encounter any issues, please refer to [this article](#)
 for further assistance.

Last updated on December 2, 2024

# Fields Character and Words Counter - Ultimate Member

Fields Character and Words Counter
==================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

The Fields Character and Words Counter plugin is a valuable addition to your Ultimate Member setup, enhancing user interactions by providing real-time character and word count functionality. Upon activation, this plugin allows effortless integration of character and word counters within the textbox and textarea fields of both the Profile and Register forms. With user-friendly configuration options, administrators can tailor the counters to suit their specific requirements. This real-time feedback not only aids users in adhering to character and word limits but also contributes to an improved overall user experience. By following the simple setup instructions, this plugin ensures seamless integration, offering a polished and informative interaction for users engaging with text fields on your website.

### Setting Character and Word Counter

Upon installing and activating the plugin, follow these steps to configure minimum and maximum characters or words for specific fields:

1.  Navigate to wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit Form.
2.  Click the "Edit" button for the desired field.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f4b892d28585006d01780/file-D8BV0egYfk.png)
    
3.  In the field manager, set the Minimum or Maximum Characters or Words by inputting the desired number in the text field. Click Update after editing the field.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f4bc200e96206bf000ffc/file-kydlINmrSM.png)
    
4.  To disable these limits, leave the text field empty or blank.
5.  Click the **"Update**" button on the form to save the changes.

With these settings, users will now encounter character or word counters on their profile or registration pages, providing real-time feedback and improving their interaction with the respective fields.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/654f4cb700e96206bf000ffd/file-mhre5wNbxO.png)

# How to set up a user role that can edit other member without wp-admin access - Ultimate Member

How to set up a user role that can edit other member without wp-admin access
============================================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

This article describes how to create a user role that can edit specific user roles without access to the wp-admin area. This may be useful if you need a person whose role is to review and approve new members.

* * *

Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles_ and click the **Add New** button to create a new user role.

Set needed permissions:

*   **Can access wp-admin?** - turn OFF
*   **Force hiding adminbar in frontend?** - turn ON
*   **Can edit other member accounts?** - turn ON
*   **Can edit these user roles only** - select roles that this person can edit, exclude important roles.
*   **Can delete other member accounts?** - turn ON if you want to allow this role to delete members.
*   **Can delete these user roles only** - select roles that this person can delete, exclude important roles.
*   **Can view other member profiles?** - turn ON

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64b13cfea9d61472afe08c15/file-OSNqDufY7j.png)

Set needed capabilities:

*   **edit\_users**
*   **read**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64b13d34d48f7f58ce2127bc/file-ooSDpQ7RCl.png)

Click the **Update Role** button to save changes.

* * *

Assign this user role to a person who has to review and approve new members.

A person with this role will see members who are waiting for approval in the members directory and can view their profiles. This person will see the gear icon that opens the profile actions menu in other member profiles.

Available actions:

*   **Approve Membership**
*   **Reject Membership**
*   **Deactivate this account**
*   **Delete this user** (if the "Can delete other member accounts?" permission is turned on)
*   **Edit Profile**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64b13d8a452d013653efbf55/file-KafERVHfkx.png)

Last updated on January 17, 2025

### Related Articles

*   [User Roles Settings](/article/103-user-roles-settings)
    
*   [How to use User Roles](/article/1590-how-to-use-user-roles)

# Custom Post Type capabilities not showing in the WP Capabilities in User Role Settings - Ultimate Member

Custom Post Type capabilities not showing in the WP Capabilities in User Role Settings
======================================================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Your CPT or Custom Post Type doesn't show in the UM WP Capabilities because there are no capabilities added to it.

1\. Please install this User Role Editor plugin: [https://wordpress.org/plugins/user-role-editor/](https://wordpress.org/plugins/user-role-editor/)

2\. Once activated, please go to WP Admin > Users > User Role Editor.

3\. Look for your custom post type. In my example, my CPT is called "Community Pantries" 

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/60924da68996210f18bda40f/file-lVHZ8oWvhl.png)

4\. Now, assign the capabilities to your CPT and then click on "Update".

5\. Go to UM User Roles > WP Capabilities. Look for your post type capabilities e.g. \`edit\_pantry\_post\` and \`edit\_published\_pantry\_post\`

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/60924e488996210f18bda414/file-360J72Cc7q.png)
