# Friends shortcodes - Ultimate Member

Friends shortcodes
==================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

This document provides instructions on how to use shortcodes of the extension [Friends](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/friends/)
.

Ultimate Member uses WordPress shortcodes to allow users to perform certain actions as well as display forms and templates. You may use shortcodes in the posts(page) content and text widgets.

**Contents**

[\[ultimatemember\_friends\]](#ultimatemember_friends)
 - Show friends list

[\[ultimatemember\_friends\_bar\]](#ultimatemember_friends_bar)
 - Show friends counter

[\[ultimatemember\_friends\_online\]](#ultimatemember_friends_online)
 - Show friends, who are online

[\[ultimatemember\_friend\_reqs\]](#ultimatemember_friend_reqs)
 - Show members, who invited this member to be a friend

[\[ultimatemember\_friend\_reqs\_sent\]](#ultimatemember_friend_reqs_sent)
 - Show members, this member invited to be a friend

Shortcodes
----------

#### \[ultimatemember\_friends\] [#](#ultimatemember_friends)

This shortcode shows the friends list.

**Attributes**

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| _Key_ | _Type_ | _Default_ | _Description_ |
| user\_id | integer | Current profile ID (on profile page)  <br>or current user ID | The user ID. |
| style | string | default | **avatars** - show lists with avatars  <br>**default** - show lists with avatars and friend bars |
| max | integer | 11  | Number of friends to display.  <br>**0** - show all friends |

**Example** - Display your friends.

`[ultimatemember_friends]`

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65aaced369979704a279a6ab/file-b7mKPhTmcx.png)

**Example** - Display friends list of another member or a specific user.

`[ultimatemember_friends user_id="3263"]`

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65aad282126ef46c7032eda9/file-miUiDkKRS5.png)

**Example** - Display other member friends list in (avatars style)

`[ultimatemember_friends user_id="3263" style="avatars"]`

#### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65aad494f1393916b8ea1a18/file-ory6x7pnQm.png)\[ultimatemember\_friends\_bar\] [#](#ultimatemember_friends_bar)

This shortcode shows friends counter.

**Attributes**

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| _Key_ | _Type_ | _Default_ | _Description_ |
| user\_id | integer | Current profile ID | The user ID. |

**Example** - Display friends counter.

`[ultimatemember_friends_bar]`

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65aad61169979704a279a6bd/file-GSL49BmUgS.png)

#### \[ultimatemember\_friends\_online\] [#](#ultimatemember_friends_online)

This shortcode lets you see which of your friends are currently online. It displays list of online friends. You need to install and activate the [online users extension](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/online-users/)
 for this feature to work.

**Attributes**

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| _Key_ | _Type_ | _Default_ | _Description_ |
| user\_id | integer | Current profile ID (on profile page)  <br>or current user ID | The user ID. |
| style | string | default | **avatars** - show lists with avatars  <br>**default** - show lists with avatars and friend bars |
| max | integer | 12  | Number of friends to display.  <br>**0** - show all friends |

**Example** - Display friends, who are online.

`[ultimatemember_friends_online]`

#### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65aade8751f0473afc686f63/file-BJNJtdD03s.png)\[ultimatemember\_friend\_reqs\] [#](#ultimatemember_friend_reqs)

This shortcode displays a list of members or users who have invited you or sent friend requests. 

**Attributes**

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| _Key_ | _Type_ | _Default_ | _Description_ |
| user\_id | integer | Current profile ID (on profile page)  <br>or current user ID | The user ID. |
| style | string | default | **avatars** - show lists with avatars  <br>**default** - show lists with avatars and member bars |
| max | integer | 999 | Number of members to display. |

**Example** - Display members, who invited you to be a friend.

`[ultimatemember_friend_reqs]`

#### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65aae1a8126ef46c7032edb8/file-eHrD0BJELz.png)\[ultimatemember\_friend\_reqs\_sent\] [#](#ultimatemember_friend_reqs_sent)

This shortcode show a list of members or users to whom you have sent friend requests.

**Attributes**

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| _Key_ | _Type_ | _Default_ | _Description_ |
| user\_id | integer | Current profile ID (on profile page)  <br>or current user ID | The user ID. |
| style | string | default | **avatars** - show lists with avatars  <br>**default** - show lists with avatars and member bars |
| max | integer | 999 | Number of members to display. |

**Example** - Display members or users, you have sent friend request to.

`[ultimatemember_friend_reqs_sent]` 

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/65aae29b126ef46c7032edbf/file-kBRrdf02WH.png)

Last updated on January 19, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [Friends Setup](/article/249-friends-setup)
    

No results found

# Groups setup - Ultimate Member

Groups setup
============

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

Groups extension allows users to create and join groups around shared topics, interests, etc., and increase user interaction on your site. This document provides instructions on how to setup [Groups extension](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/groups)
.

**Contents**

*   [Settings](#settings)
    *   [Extensions settings](#settings_common)
        
    *   [Profile Menu settings](#settings_menu)
        
    *   [Pages settings](#settings_pages)
        
*   [User Role settings](#user_role_settings)
    
*   [Manage groups](#manage_groups)
    *   [Add a new group](#group_new)
        
    *   [A group settings](#group)
        *   [Settings section](#group_settings)
            
        *   [Invites settings section](#group_invites_settings)
            
        *   [Add new members section](#group_new-member)
            
        *   [Manage members section](#group_members)
            
*   [Group page on the frontend](#frontend)
    
*   [Notifications](#notifications)
    *   [Email notifications](#email_notifications)
        
    *   [Real-time notifications](#realtime_notifications)
        

Settings [#](#settings)

------------------------

### Extensions settings [#](#settings_common)

[](#settings_common)
Once you activate the extension, you will see the **Groups** tab on the page _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions_. On this page, you can find the following options:

*   Edit Groups slug, Category slug & Tag
*   Turn on/off group avatars "Show group avatars"
*   Setup the number of displayed posts for the mobile/desktop view
*   Setup comments order
*   Setup number of initial comments/replies to display per post

Image - Settings on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > Groups_

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/618b77152b380503dfe03251/file-JzVGiB70PF.png)

### Profile Menu settings [#](#settings_menu)

The extension adds settings to the page _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Appearance > Profile Menu_. These settings allow you to manage the "Groups" item in the profile menu:

*   **Groups Tab** – Allows enabling/disabling the "Groups" item in the Profile menu.
*   **Who can see Groups Tab?** – Manage access to the "Groups" tab in the Profile menu

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5eec821d04286306f80573a7/file-pNXyylAtiy.png)

### Pages settings [#](#settings_pages)

The extension adds settings to the page _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > General > Pages_. These settings allow you to manage required pages:

*   **Groups** - Displays all public and private groups
*   **My Groups** - Displays current user groups
*   **Create New Group** - Displays a form to create a group

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5ea2ed3d04286364bc98f393/file-MUZbIGD32V.png)

Page **Groups** contains the [\[ultimatemember\_groups\]](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1495-groups-shortcodes#ultimatemember_groups)
 shortcode that shows a list of groups. Members can use this page to search a group, see a group info, join a group.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/620419c468cd260cc2d39796/file-tD60xJzd1j.png)

Page **My Groups** contains the [\[ultimatemember\_my\_groups\]](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1495-groups-shortcodes#ultimatemember_my_groups)
 shortcode that shows a list of groups, which current member has joined.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62041b0c68cd260cc2d3979d/file-Gjk2dQU84z.png)

Page **Create New Group** contains the [\[ultimatemember\_group\_new\]](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1495-groups-shortcodes#ultimatemember_group_new)
 shortcode that gives members the opportunity to create a group. The user, who created the group, is the first group member and the group "Administrator".

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62041b6039e5d05141b68086/file-qRfir2gHBF.png)

The extension creates pages automatically. You can recreate pages manually if they were lost in _wp-admin > Pages_. Once pages are created, you should configure them using options on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > General > Pages_.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62041d8768cd260cc2d397b8/file-6p9rmPJI2y.png)

User Role settings [#](#user_role_settings)

--------------------------------------------

The extension adds the **Groups** section to the user role settings. Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles_, choose the user role and scroll down to see this section. You'll see these settings:

*   **Turn off creation group?** - turn on this setting to deny creating groups for this role.

Image - The **Groups** section in the user role settings.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6489a1a87f8c2575e3544dc1/file-owvQUSRRZg.png)

Manage groups [#](#manage_groups)

----------------------------------

The extension adds the **Groups** section to the WordPress admin menu where you can view existing groups, add a new group or edit group settings.

You can view and manage group categories on _wp-admin > Groups > Group Categories_.

You can view and manage group tags on _wp-admin > Groups > Group Tags_.

Image - The **Groups** section in the WordPress admin menu and Groups table.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64899de55fc2fe6b6dc5fe80/file-OpTV2c2aJM.png)

### Add a new group [#](#group_new)

Click the **Add New Group** button above the Groups table to add a new group. Configure a group settings and click the **Add Group** button.

### A group settings [#](#group)

Group settings are divided into 4 main widgets: **Settings**, **Invites settings**, **Add New Members**, **Manage Members**.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62040e0e2130e5169468594e/file-rmCdXIoCPL.png)

#### Settings section [#](#group_settings)

*   **Privacy** - set privacy settings for your group. Options:
    *   **Public** - Any site member can join this group. Group content and discussions are visible to any site member.
    *   **Public for Role** - Members with specific role(s) can join this group. Group content and discussions are visible to members with specific role(s).
    *   **Private** - Only members who request membership and are accepted can join the group. Group content and discussions are visible to members of the group.
    *   **Hidden** - Only members who are invited can join the group. Group content and discussions are visible to members of the group. This group is not listed in the groups' directory or search results.
*   **Role list** - (optional, appears if "Public for Role" is chosen in the Privacy) limit access by user role.
*   **Post Moderation** - enable/disable post moderation by admins or group moderators.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5ebbe807042863474d1a9fc2/file-5DL5capkvV.png)

#### Invites settings section [#](#group_invites_settings)

*   **Enable Invites feature** - enable/disable invites for the group.
*   **Who can invite members to the group?** - select who can invite new group members. Options.
    *   All Group Members
    *   Group Administrators & Moderators only
    *   Group Administrators only
*   **Enable Invites search** - enable/disable searching tool.
*   **Enable Invites filters** - enable/disable filtering tool.
*   **Choose field(s) to enable in search** - select fields for filtering if "Enable Invites filters" is enabled.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5ebbe812042863474d1a9fc3/file-pF5x12Q4ao.png)

How it looks like on the group page:

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6204113e39e5d05141b68048/file-kxUt4m5EnJ.png)

#### Add new members section [#](#group_new-member)

You can add new members to the group by searching for registered users and clicking on the "Add member" button.

Step 1 - Search a member

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5bf40cbb04286304a71c700f/file-QsdH3JqPCd.jpg)

Step 2 - Add a member

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/620412ab2130e51694685968/file-qg0zomlYtq.png)

#### Manage members section [#](#group_members)

Manage members section allows you to manage group members:

*   Change member status - Hover over the status of the member to select the following status from the drop-down menu: Pending Admin Review, Pending Member Review, Approved, Rejected, and Blocked
*   Assign/change member role - Hover over the group role of the member to select the following group role from the drop-down menu: Administrator, Moderator, Member and Banned.
*   Send invite or expel members from the group

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/620412ea025ca67522c7985e/file-w0A9n8DYej.png)

Group page on the frontend [#](#frontend)

------------------------------------------

On the group page on the frontend 3 main sections are available:

*   **Discussions** tab represents the main group discussion feed
*   **Members** tab displays all group members
*   **Send invites** tab can be used to send invites to other members

Image - A group page example.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62041685025ca67522c79875/file-p9syhedkAK.png)

Admin users or group moderators can access group settings on the frontend by clicking the "gear" icon on the upper right side of the group page. All group settings can be changed on the frontend by group admins or moderators.

Image - A group frontend settings example.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/620417dd025ca67522c7987d/file-EV0Y1y0YK1.png)

Notifications [#](#notifications)

----------------------------------

The extension has email notifications and real-time\* notifications.

**Note:** Real-time notifications require the extension [Ultimate Member - Real-time Notifications](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/real-time-notifications/)
.

### Email notifications [#](#email_notifications)

Email templates:

*   **Groups - Approve Member Email** - Send the user an email when the user is approved to a group
*   **Groups - Join Request Email** - Send the user an email when the user has requested to join their group
*   **Groups - Invite Member Email** - Send the user an email when the user has been invited to join a group
*   **Groups - New post** - Send the user an email when someone posts on the group wall
*   **Groups - New comment** - Send the user an email when someone comments on the group wall

The Administrator can edit email templates: enable, disable, change subject or body. Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Email_, find the needed email template, and click the button "Edit" (gear icon). You'll see email template settings. Change what you want and save changes.

Image - Screen _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Email_.__

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5e99fdb404286364bc989023/file-8btOw1Gyds.png)

Each member can enable or disable email notifications **Groups - New post** and **Groups - New comment** using settings on the page _Account > Notifications_. These settings are disabled by default.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5eb58423042863474d1a66ae/file-U35ikv5QAF.png)

### Real-time notifications [#](#realtime_notifications)

Real-time templates:

*   **Groups - Approve Member** - When my group requests have been approved
*   **Groups - Join Request** - When a user requested to join their group
*   **Groups - Invite Member** - When a member has been invited to join a group
*   **Groups - Change Group Role** - When my group roles have been changed

Image - Real-time notification example.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6204189f025ca67522c79880/file-jrtP0JXzGr.png)

Each member can enable or disable real-time notifications using settings on the page _Account > Web notifications_.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/618b7e2b12c07c18afde6194/file-Aix2c5A1o8.png)

Last updated on January 15, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [Groups shortcodes](/article/1495-groups-shortcodes)
    

No results found

# Ultimate Member - JobBoardWP integration - Ultimate Member

Ultimate Member - JobBoardWP integration
========================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Overview

[Ultimate Member - JobBoardWP](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/jobboardwp/)
 integration is an easy-to-use and lightweight plugin that enables you to add integration between job board functionality and user profiles to your website. This document provides instructions on integrating JobBoardWP with Ultimate Member using the JobBoardWP integration extension.

> **Note**: You have to install and activate plugins JobBoardWP and Ultimate Member - JobBoardWP for the extension to work.  

#### **Contents  

*   [Settings](#settings)
    *   [User Role Settings](#userrolesettings)
        
    *   [Profile Menu Settings](#profilemenusettings)
        
    *   [Social Activity Settings](#socialactivitysettings)
        
    *   [Private Messaging Settings](#privatemessagingsettings)
        
    *   [User Bookmarks Settings](#userbookmarkssettings)
        
    *   [Pages Settings](#pagessettings)
        
    *   [Configure Notifications](#configurenotifsettings)
        
    *   [Real-Time Notification](#realtimenotif)
        
    *   [Verified User Settings](#verifiedusersettings)
        

**

### Settings [#](#settings)

Main extension's settings are placed at the page \[Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions >JobBoardWP\]  

*   Account Tab - Show or hide an account tab that shows the jobs dashboard.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6194db0fefc78d0553e5b00f/file-SqamgyUXWk.png)

The extension adds a tab to the account page for users with employer roles. By clicking on the ellipsis " **...**" symbol, you can edit, delete, mark as filled, continue submitting, and submit the jobs again from the account tab.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6204202868cd260cc2d397d0/file-zXUWDHGev1.png)

### User Roles settings[#](#settings)

You can enable or disable job tabs in the user's profile. User's posted jobs are displayed in the job tabs.  
\[Ultimate Member > User Roles > Edit Role and see the JobBoardWP section\]

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6194fcf9efc78d0553e5b0b5/file-2wNvFFpCRJ.png)

### Profile Menu settings[#](#profilemenusettings)

The extension adds settings to the page \[Ultimate Member > Settings > Appearance > Profile Menu\]. These settings allow you to manage the "Jobs Tab" in the profile menu:

*   Jobs Tab – Allows enabling/disabling the "Jobs Tab" in the Profile menu.
*   Who can see Jobs Tab? – Manage access to the "Jobs Tab" in the Profile menu.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6194ff5f2b380503dfe05ce6/file-k89LPF2iww.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62041f0fd86136157d9a28c8/file-oVSimMR3kF.png)

### Social Activity settings[#](#socialactivitysettings)

You can enable or disable activity posts regarding jobs in wp-admin>Ultimate Member> Extension> Social Activity

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62042ab939e5d05141b680ed/file-UIl7DkdIBD.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62042f8b2130e51694685a40/file-Wg5V9ACKxm.png)

_Note: Social Activity settings require the extension "[Ultimate Member - Social Activity](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/social-activity/)
."_

### Private Messaging settings[#](#privatemessagingsettings)

Enable or disable the message button in an individual job post to directly message the job author in wp-admin> Ultimate Member> Settings> Extension> Private Messaging.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62043128025ca67522c79934/file-vOzmIVZo6e.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/620431972130e51694685a52/file-qkMQuC7ZKr.png)

_Note: This setting requires the extension "[Ultimate Member - Private Messages](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/private-messages/)
."_

### User Bookmarks settings[#](#userbookmarkssettings)

Users can individually bookmark a job, to enable it go to wp-admin> Ultimate Member> Settings> User Bookmarks> Enable bookmark. Select the "Jobs" from the scroll options.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/620434ba2130e51694685a66/file-3isqvW5Cs0.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/620436fe68cd260cc2d3986d/file-nSeBRCnzqH.png)

Once the user bookmarked a job, they will view it in the bookmarks tab in their profile.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6204372768cd260cc2d3986e/file-6K98IKhtBC.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/620437bf68cd260cc2d39877/file-AwZ9nOgJfR.png)

You can also enable bookmarks on each page select the "Pages" from the scroll options.

_Note: These settings require the extension "[Ultimate Member - User Bookmarks](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/user-bookmarks/)
."_

### Pages settings[#](#pagessettings)

Once you install the  JobBoardWP integration extension, you can edit the Jobs, Jobs Dashboard, and Post Job pages in  **\[wp-admin> Pages> Edit Page\]**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6195d3d1efc78d0553e5b547/file-Pe4p7sJqBX.png)

These page settings allow users to enable or disable content restriction and UM User Bookmarks. Click on the checkbox to enable the settings.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62042394025ca67522c798d1/file-QdTpmH7Dil.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62043ae7025ca67522c7995a/file-du7Y11ixNg.png)

### Configure notifications[#](#configurenotifsettings)

The UM- JobBoardWP integration has a real-time\* notifications.  

_Note: Real-time notifications require the extension "_ [Ultimate Member - Real-time Notifications](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/real-time-notifications/)
."     

**\[wp-admin> Ultimate Member> Settings> Extensions> Notifications\]**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6195e11812c07c18afde910e/file-Bgnjqq9fGc.png)

### Real-time notifications[#](#realtimenotif)

*   'Your job is approved' - When your job post requests have been approved.
*   'Your job is expired' - When your job posts have expired.

Each member can enable or disable real-time notifications using settings on the page \[Account > Web notifications\]  

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6195e5869ccf62287e5f79ab/file-fKLoff9z9a.png)

### Verified User Settings[#](#verifiedusersettings)

If you only want verified users can apply in job posts, you can enable the settings in wp-admin>Ultimate Member>Settings> Extensions>Verified user.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62043f242130e51694685a9a/file-Z9FQTDr3ua.png)Only the verified user can see the **Apply for job** button when the setting is enabled.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/62043f3639e5d05141b68162/file-ml7ef8bkQn.png)

_Note: Verified User settings require the extension "_ [Ultimate Member - Verified Users](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/verified-users/)
."     

Last updated on March 14, 2023

No results found

# How to debug MailChimp error - Ultimate Member

How to debug MailChimp error
============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

This document provides instructions on how to detect and debug errors that may appear when a member subscribes to the MailChimp audience using the [MailChimp extension](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/mailchimp/)
. Please read the [MailChimp Setup](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/82-mailchimp-setup)
 first.

**Contents**

*   [Debugging](#debugging)
    *   [Update plugins](#debug_step_010)
        
    *   [Enable requests log](#debug_step_020)
        
    *   [Verify fields merge settings](#debug_step_030)
        
    *   [Test manual update](#debug_step_040)
        
    *   [Investigate the Requests log](#debug_step_060)
        
*   [Common MailChimp errors](#errors)
    *   [Invalid Resource - Your merge fields were invalid](#Your-merge-fields-were-invalid)
        
    *   [Forgotten Email Not Subscribed](#Forgotten-Email-Not-Subscribed)
        
    *   [Member In Compliance State](#Member-In-Compliance-State)
        

Debugging [#](#debugging)

--------------------------

#### Update plugins [#](#debug_step_010)

Update the "Ultimate Member" and "Ultimate Member - MailChimp" plugins to the latest version. You can update plugins automatically or update it manually from the archive.

Image - How to update plugin automatically

![Plugins, Installed Plugins](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5dac0def2c7d3a7e9ae2c503/file-ZARPsEs6kh.png)

Follow instructions in the [documentation](https://wordpress.org/support/article/managing-plugins/#upload-via-wordpress-admin)
 to update the plugin manually from the .zip archive.

#### Enable requests log [#](#debug_step_020)

The extension may save information about external API requests. Turn on the **Enable requests log** setting on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > MailChimp_ to enable this feature.

Image - How to enable the **Requests log**.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/63ee1c457a31233f59f6dd1b/file-zW1vc6qNJP.png)

#### Verify fields merge settings [#](#debug_step_030)

The request to add/update a contact fails if fields data are wrong or incomplete. Follow recommendations in [Field type restrictions](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/82-mailchimp-setup#field_rules)
 to avoid problems.

Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp > Edit MailChimp connection_ and verify that:

*   All required audience fields are merged with equal required fields in the registration form.
*   The **address** audience field is not used or address data collected in the registration form are complete.
*   The **drop down** and **radio buttons** audience fields are merged with proper form fields with exactly the same options (choices).

The **Merge User Meta** widget has several tools that help you to configure fields relationship and avoid mistakes:

*   required fields marked by the **!** icon
*   additional information about field type and choices may be seen under the **?** icon
*   a **warning** appears under the field if the field merged wrong. Hover over the **?** icon to see details.

Image - The **Merge User Meta** widget in the connection settings.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/604a1ba5207e3b1188e1b1d6/file-likbKERXAM.png)

Image - A warning in the **Merge User Meta** widget.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/636e9dd83fc88c6e0f00684c/file-49ONp5gHbo.png)

#### Test manual update [#](#debug_step_040)

Try to update a contact using the "Email Newsletters" tool in the account tab Notifications. Error message appears in if the update fails for some reason. Look at the **Requests log** to see error details.

Image - Error message in the account tab Notifications.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/63ee174e1aa7be0e19ffd43f/file-1EyPn0uQyH.png)

#### Investigate the Requests log [#](#debug_step_060)

The extension may save requests to the MailChimp server. You can overview the **Requests log** on the extension's settings page. Use the log to debug MailChimp API errors. Requests with errors have the red ERROR label. You should see the error detail after the ERROR label.

Image - An error detail in the **Requests log** on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > MailChimp_.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/63ee1e13e669fb108d6ff2b6/file-gCXyZaq2Du.png)

Common MailChimp errors [#](#errors)

-------------------------------------

### Invalid Resource - Your merge fields were invalid. [#](#Your-merge-fields-were-invalid)

This error appears if data sent for merge fields are incorrect or there is no value for the required field.

### Forgotten Email Not Subscribed [#](#Forgotten-Email-Not-Subscribed)

MailChimp does not allow re-subscribe permanently deleted contacts by API. That's why we don't recommend using the "Permanently delete" action on account removal. You should go to your MailChimp account and add this contact manually.

### Member In Compliance State [#](#Member-In-Compliance-State)

MailChimp adds an email address to the ignore list if a member uses the link "unsubscribe from this list" in the email footer.

Image - The unsubscribe link

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5f898d444cedfd0017dd26b2/file-COR04F5F8m.png)

This email address can't be resubscribed via API. You should see an error message if you try to add this contact using the "Email Newsletters" tool in the account tab Notifications. We can't influence this from the site. A member can resubscribe by clicking the button "Subscribe" in the email "You are now unsubscribed" that MailChimp sends after the contact is unsubscribed.

Image - The email "You are now unsubscribed"

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5f898d61cff47e001a58f1c6/file-XbVKN8kn8k.png)

# MailChimp Setup - Ultimate Member

MailChimp Setup
===============

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

This document provides instructions on how to integrate MailChimp with Ultimate Member using the [MailChimp extension](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/mailchimp/)
.

The extension adds contacts to your MailChimp audience when users register on your website. Once you have contacts with data you can start segmenting your audience and create automations or campaigns. Read MailChimp documentation about [segmenting](https://mailchimp.com/help/getting-started-with-segments/)
, [automation](https://mailchimp.com/help/create-customer-journey/)
 and [campaigns](https://mailchimp.com/help/getting-started-with-campaigns/)
 if you're new to MailChimp.

**Contents**

*   [Getting started](#getting-started)
    
*   [Get the MailChimp API Key](#api_key)
    
*   [Create an audience](#create_audience)
    
*   [Configure settings](#settings)
    *   [Extension settings](#extra_settings)
        *   [Requests log](#requests_log)
            
    *   [User Role settings](#role_settings)
        
*   [Create a connection](#connection)
    *   [Create a connection with the audience](#add_audience)
        
    *   [General connection settings](#connection_settings)
        
    *   [Configure fields](#fields)
        *   [Audience fields](#audience_fields)
            
        *   [Merge User Meta widget](#merge_user_meta)
            
        *   [Field type restrictions](#field_rules)
            
*   [Configure behavior on registration](#registration_form)
    
*   [Account features](#manage_newsletters)
    
*   [Manual syncing](#syncing)
    *   [Sync from the dashboard widget](#dashboard_widget)
        
    *   [Bulk subscribe for newsletters from the Users table](#users_table_bulk_subscribe)
        

Getting started [#](#getting-started)

--------------------------------------

Main steps to configure the extension:

1.  Go to [MailChimp](https://mailchimp.com/)
     and create an account.
2.  Create a MailChimp audience.
3.  Copy MailChimp API key.
4.  Go to your site and install the [MailChimp extension](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/mailchimp/)
    .
5.  Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > MailChimp_ and paste your MailChimp API key into the field "MailChimp API Key". Save changes.
6.  Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp_ and create a connection with the audience. Configure the connection.
7.  Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit Form (register)_ and add the MailChimp field to the registration form if needed.

Get the MailChimp API Key [#](#api_key)

----------------------------------------

Follow these steps to create your MailChimp account and [get your MailChimp API key](https://mailchimp.com/help/about-api-keys/)
:

*   Go to the [MailChimp website](http://mailchimp.com/)
     and [create an account](https://login.mailchimp.com/signup/)
     or [login](https://login.mailchimp.com/)
     if you already have an account.
*   Click the user icon to open the user menu then click the **Account and billing** item.
*   On the account page click the **Extras** menu item then click the **API keys** subitem.
*   Scroll down to the **Your API Keys** section and click the **Create A Key** button.
*   Enter the **API Key Name** and click the **Generate Key** button.
*   Copy your API key and click the **Done** button.

Once you have an API key, go to your website and paste it into the  **MailChimp API Key** setting on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > MailChimp._

Image - MailChimp user menu. ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678dd947f36f833b3003dc28/file-nkak87lKF1.png)

Image -  **Overview** screen, the "API Keys" menu item.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678dd9addd82394f253b5e2a/file-GsoaF3Vmft.png)Image - **Extras** screen, the **Your API Keys** section.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678dda017192f72090648589/file-kFDaVPg1km.png)

Image - **Name New API Key** screen.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678de117dbf3a56febdeb599/file-ojjBR5JAds.png)

Image - **Copy New API Key** screen.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678de3be58e23c36b083643d/file-R6luNV2dsK.png)

Image - The **MailChimp API Key** setting on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions > MailChimp_.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678de47796f8615ee5da2823/file-dDGwh2EffA.png)

Create an audience [#](#create_audience)

-----------------------------------------

For the integration between MailChimp and Ultimate Member, you need to [create a MailChimp audience](https://mailchimp.com/help/create-audience/)
. Please do the following:

*   Click the "Audience" item in the MailChimp menu.
*   On the audiences page click the "Create audience" button. MailChimp allows you to create the first audience for free. The "Create audience" button is hidden if you reached a limit of audiences allowed for your plan.
*   On the create audience page fill in all the details and click save.
*   You new audience will be created.

| General audience settings | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **Audience name** | Required |
| **Audience ID** | Generated automatically |
| **Enable double opt-in** | Optional. [See details here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1588-enable-double-opt-in) |
| **Enable reCAPTCHA** | Not used by the API |
| **Default From name** | Recommended |
| **Default From email address** | Recommended |
| **Default Email subject** | Recommended |

Image - **Audience name and campaign defaults** screen, the audience settings.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678de849dbf3a56febdeb5a1/file-ecOajvH5YN.png)

Configure settings [#](#settings)

----------------------------------

### Extension settings [#](#extra_settings)

Once you activate the extension, you will see the **MailChimp** tab on the page _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Settings > Extensions_. On this tab, you can find these settings:

| Setting | Description |
| --- | --- |
| MailChimp API Key | This field requires you to input the API Key copied from your MailChimp account.  The **MailChimp API key** allows **Ultimate Member** to integrate seamlessly with **WordPress**, syncing your user data with your MailChimp audiences. This integration helps automate email marketing and user management directly within your WordPress site. |
| Account delete action | Specifies what action to take when a user account is deleted. Choose from the following options:  <br><br>*   **Do nothing**: the contact with the audience will not be changed.<br>*   **Move to archive:** the contact in the audience will be archived.<br>*   **Permanently delete:** the contact will be removed from the audience forever.<br>*   **Unsubscribe member:** the contact in the audience will be marked as "Unsubscribed". _Recommended._<br>Actions "Move to archive", "Permanently delete", and "Unsubscribe member" stop mailing.  <br><br>**Warning:** The "Permanently delete" action removes a contact from the audience forever. Permanently deleted users cannot be resubscribed. Don't use this if you're not sure. |
| Account unapprove action | (Optional) [See details here](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1588-enable-double-opt-in)<br>. Specifies what action to take when an account's status is unapproved or deactivated. |
| Groups and Tags view | Defines how to display **Groups** and **Tags** in the user profile field. You can choose from the following display options:  <br><br>*   **Checkboxes**: Displays groups and tags as checkboxes, allowing the user to select multiple options at once.<br>*   **Multi-Select**: Allows for selecting multiple options, but displayed as a dropdown list instead of checkboxes. |
| Enable language field | Allows members to select their preferred language for receiving newsletters. When enabled, an additional option **Default language** will appear. |
| Default language | You can select the default language from the dropdown list, with **English** as the default option. This ensures members receive newsletters in their preferred language. |
| Enable requests cache | Improves performance by caching MailChimp API requests, reducing the need for repeated calls to the API. This setting must be enabled to access the **Cache timeout** option. |
| Cache timeout, s | (Optional) Specifies how long cached API requests should be stored, in seconds. The default value is **600 seconds** (10 minutes). A longer timeout reduces API calls but may delay updates to MailChimp data. |
| Enable requests log | Saves detailed information about all requests and responses sent to the MailChimp API. This feature is useful for configuring and debugging the extension. Enabling this option reveals additional settings: **Log all responses** and **Requests log**. |
| Log all responses | (Optional) Logs responses for all API requests, including successful ones. Note that responses for failed requests are always logged by default. |
| Requests log | (Optional) Displays the saved log of API requests and responses. The log includes filtering and search functionality to find specific entries. You can also clear or refresh the log as needed. |
| Blocked Email Addresses | Specify a list of email addresses and domains that you want to block from being added to the audience. Add each email or domain on a new line. Addresses listed here cannot be added to the audience, and existing contacts will be unsubscribed during the next update if automatic sync is enabled. To block an entire domain, use a wildcard format such as `*@domain.com`. |

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678ea11f41e76e45e418fea1/file-iW7a7MoqAJ.png)

#### Requests log [#](#requests_log)

The requests log appears if the **Enable requests log** setting is turned on. It displays the time, status, method, request path, arguments, and referrer for each request to the MailChimp API. The extension stores a response for failed requests by default. If the **Log all responses** setting is turned on, the extension stores a response for all requests.

You can find the error details in the **RESPONSE** data. This information helps to configure and debug the extension.

You can also filter and search the requests log using the following dropdown options:

*   **All requests**: View all recorded requests.
*   **Only requests with error**: View only the requests that encountered an error.
*   **Only requests to add or update**: View requests related to adding or updating records.

Additionally, you can view important requests for a specific user by clicking **View log** in the **MailChimp** column under **wp-admin > Users**.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678ea1d08e63e96f9ad60614/file-9psjqLDWT8.png)

### User Role settings [#](#role_settings)

The extension adds settings to manage what members can edit from their accounts. Go to  _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles_ and choose the user role to modify. You will see the "Edit Role" page. Scroll down to the "MailChimp" section:

*   **Can manage groups?**
*   **Can manage tags?**
*   **Can create tags?**

Image - Settings on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles > Edit Role.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678dfc48dd82394f253b5e6b/file-TTmmxo5Y86.png)_

Create a connection [#](#connection)

-------------------------------------

### Create a connection with the audience [#](#add_audience)

Follow these steps to create a connection with the audience:

*   Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp_ and click the **Add New** button.
*   Set the title. 
*   Select the MailChimp audience in the **Choose an audience** dropdown.
*   Click the **Create** button.

Image - The **MailChimp connections** table on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678dfd9096f8615ee5da2846/file-ZmIBGwlW5o.png)_

Image - The **Choose an audience** dropdown on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp > Add New.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678dff097192f720906485d4/file-El37PvQsT2.png)_

### General connection settings [#](#connection_settings)

| **Setting** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| Mailing audience ID | Show MailChimp audience ID. |
| Enable this connection | Turn on or off this connection globally. |
| Enable double opt-in | Send contacts an opt-in confirmation email when they subscribe to your audience. This setting can enable the [double opt-in](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1588-enable-double-opt-in)<br> feature if it isn't enabled in the audience settings. This setting is inactive if the [double opt-in](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1588-enable-double-opt-in)<br> feature is enabled in the audience settings. |
| Who can subscribe to this audience | Select which roles can subscribe to the audience. Members who cannot subscribe to the audience won't see this audience in their accounts. |
| Automatically sync contacts | Syncs a contact on registration, profile update, or role change. |
| Default interests | _Optional._ Shown if the audience has groups. Defines group interests to automatically add a new member to these groups if the "Automatically sync contacts" option is enabled. Each group has a separate setting to select group interests. |
| Default interests for existing contacts | How to apply default interests when updating existing contacts. |
| Default tags | _Optional._ Shown if the audience has tags. Defines tags to automatically add these tags to a new member if the "Automatically sync contacts" option is enabled. |
| Default tags for existing contacts | How to apply default tags when updating existing contacts. |
| Marketing Permission | _Optional._ Shown if the audience setting "Enable GDPR fields" is enabled. |

**\* Note:** The extension adds new members to the audience after the email confirmation if the option "double opt-in" is enabled.

**\*\* Note:** The extension adds new members to the audience after approval if the option "Automatically sync contacts" is enabled and their user role is selected in the option "Who can subscribe to this audience". The extension does not add new members before approval. The extension does not add new members if their role is not selected.

Image - The **Setup audience** section on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp > Edit MailChimp connection._

_![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e087c2bc0823332f676d4/file-k79WGVPdfL.png)_

### Configure fields [#](#fields)

Once you set up the connection and updated the page the  **Merge User Meta** section will appear. This widget syncs form fields with audience fields.

#### Audience fields [#](#audience_fields)

The audience should be set up in a specific way to get user data from the site. You have to create audience fields first. Do the following:

*   Go to audiences and select the desired audience.
*   Go to _Audience > Settings > Audience fields and \*|MERGE|\* tags._
*   You will notice that the form includes Email Address, First Name and Last Name. You can use these fields.
*   Click the **Add A Field** button to add more fields. Select a field type and configure field's settings.
*   Save Changes.

Image - **Audience fields and \*|MERGE|\* tags** screen, the audience fields.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e0b847d099a7df55f2cf5/file-fMWrrf4tOu.png)

#### Merge User Meta widget [#](#merge_user_meta)

Once you create the needed audience fields go back to the connection and refresh the page. You'll see the widget **Merge User Meta**, which syncs form fields with the audience fields. In this widget, audience fields are shown on the left and form fields are shown on the right. You should set relationships between audience fields and form fields created on your site. Click the **Update** button to save changes.

Pay attention to the required fields and choices for the "radio buttons" and "drop-down" field types. Synchronization would fail if one of the required fields is empty. Synchronization may fail if the values of the "radio buttons" or "drop-down" field type doesn't match.

The widget has tools that help you to configure field relationships and avoid mistakes:

*   required fields marked by the **icon (!)**
*   information about field type and choices may be seen under the **icon (?)**
*   a **warning** appears under the field if the configuration is wrong

Image - The **Merge User Meta** section on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp > Edit MailChimp connection.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e0dd27d099a7df55f2cf7/file-y7p3isyDw3.png)_

#### Field type restrictions [#](#field_rules)

The  **address** field in MailChimp is a complex field that consists of several subfields. You have to fill all required subfields or disable address field. Rules for address subfields:

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |  
| _Subfield_ | _Required_ | _Recommendation_ | _Example_ |
| Street Address | Yes | Must be merged with the required text field in the registration form | 20 W 34th St |
| Address line 2 | No  | Should be merged with the optional text field in the registration form | next floor |
| City | Yes | Must be merged with the required text field in the registration form | New York |
| State/Prov/Region | Depends on country | It is empty or text if there are no states in the country. It is a code of the state if the country has states. | NY  |
| Postal/Zip | Yes | Must be merged with the required text field in the registration form | 10001 |
| Country | Yes | Must be merged with the required dropdown field that contains country codes | US  |

MailChimp does not support **multi-select** field types. However, multi-select form fields can be synced with **text** fields, where selected options are combined into a single string, with each option separated by a comma. For example, a "Sports" field with multiple selections could be synced as "Football, Basketball, Tennis."

To target specific contacts for a mailing campaign, MailChimp uses the "Segment" feature. You can create a segment by filtering based on the values stored in the text field containing multiple options. Simply use the **"contains"** rule to match the desired values.

Image - The **Segment builder** with the **contains** rule.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e1022da723500ee30c65e/file-LZLqhAQIqV.png)

The **dropdown** and **radio button** fields in MailChimp can be synced with corresponding UM form fields (Dropdown and Radio) if the form field choices match the options in the audience field. However, we do not recommend using these field types, as synchronization can break if an option is added or removed on one side but not the other. It’s better to sync Dropdown and Radio UM form fields with a **text audience field**.

To simplify [role syncing](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1587-how-to-sync-the-user-role)
, we've added two virtual **role** fields that can be synced with a **text** field in the audience:

*   **Role slug**: Returns the role ID (e.g., "super\_editor").
*   **Role title**: Returns the role title (e.g., "Super Editor").

Configure behavior on registration [#](#registration_form)

-----------------------------------------------------------

There are two ways to handle members on registration:

*   **Auto** - Members are added to the audience after registration and approval if the **Automatically sync contacts** setting is enabled and their user role is selected in the **Who can subscribe to this audience** setting. A field in the registration form is non needed to use Auto mode.
*   **Manual** - Add the **MailChimp** field to the registration form to allow members to sign up for newsletters manually. This field is visible in the registration form if the user role assigned on registration is selected in the **Who can subscribe to this audience** setting.

**Note:** There is no way to combine auto and manual modes together. Manual mode has a higher priority. The extension uses data from the MailChimp field if the field exists in the registration form ignoring default interests and tags.

Follow these steps to add a field to the registration form:

*   Go to _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit Form (Register)._
*   Click the "+" icon to add the new field.
*   Select the **MailChimp** field.
*   Set the **title and label,** then select the **connection** from the dropdown.
*   Enable additional options if you need them:
    *   **Checked by default** - The checkbox appears checked.
    *   **Manage groups** - Additional field to select groups appears below the checkbox. Default groups are used if disabled.
    *   **Manage tags**: An additional field to select tags appears below the checkbox. Default tags are used if disabled.
    *   **Manage language**: If enabled, this option allows users to select and manage their preferred language from their account form.
    *   **Checkbox label**: This setting allows you to customize the label text for the MailChimp checkbox field. You can modify the default label to better suit your form's needs or provide more specific instructions for users.
*   Click the **Update** button to save changes. The field should appear in the form.

Image - The MailChimp field in the Form Builder on  _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit Form.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e1e3fe035ce61f18456fb/file-tZqxIgbtvv.png)_

Image - The MailChimp field options on  _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Forms > Edit Form > Edit Field_.

### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e1dcae035ce61f18456f9/file-1Yy0g8HnyR.png)Account features [#](#manage_newsletters)

Members can manage their newsletters in the account tab Notifications. Members can subscribe or unsubscribe, and manage groups and tags (optional, depends on the role settings).

Image - The **Email Newsletters** section on _Account > Notifications.  
_

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e3ab76182312736de34f4/file-FAhrT7YjW9.png)An error message appears if the update fails for some reason. Follow the instructions in the article [How to debug MailChimp error](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/1529-how-to-debug-mailchimp-error)
 to fix a problem.

Image - Error message on _Account > Notifications.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/679769772dfc722f4cbd26ae/file-kh6WKe6mG1.png)_

Manual syncing [#](#syncing)

-----------------------------

### Sync from the dashboard widget [#](#dashboard_widget)

The plugin adds the MailChimp widget to the Ultimate Member dashboard. This widget displays statistical information and provides tools for manual bulk syncing:

#### Bulk Subscribe or Unsubscribe

This tool is used to select existing site users (by role and status) and manually subscribe (add) them to the audience or unsubscribe them. Users who were previously added to the audience will have their data updated. You can also use this tool to add or remove contacts.

**Steps:**

1.  Select role and/or status and click the **"Scan now"** button. The tool searches for members matching the criteria and proceeds to the next step if members are found.
2.  Select the audience and click **"Subscribe"** or **"Unsubscribe"**.

#### Update Audience Contacts

The **Update Audience Contacts** tool updates contact data (fields) in the selected audience.

*   This tool does not add new contacts.
*   It shows detailed results for each updated contact, including error details if a contact update fails.

Use this tool to ensure existing contact fields are up-to-date.

#### Import Users from MailChimp

This tool allows you to import users directly from your MailChimp account into your Ultimate Member site. It includes the following options:

| **Option** | **Description** |
| --- | --- |
| **Select Connection** | Choose a specific MailChimp audience to import contacts from, ensuring the correct subscribers are added or updated. |
| **Import Only "Subscribed" Contacts** | Restrict the import to MailChimp contacts with a "subscribed" status to maintain a clean and relevant user base. |
| **Update Existing Users** | Synchronize and update data for users already present on your site based on MailChimp information. |
| **Create New Users** | Add new users to your site using data from MailChimp subscribers. |
| **Run Import** | Execute the import process to sync MailChimp contacts with your site users. |

Image - The MailChimp widget on  _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > Dashboard.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e921ad624882e7e78ec1e/file-0JiLljBnIt.png)_

### Bulk subscribe for newsletters from the Users table [#](#users_table_bulk_subscribe)

The plugin adds tools for bulk Subscribe or Unsubscribe to the Users table. These tools work like bulk actions. Select the users you want to update, select a connection, and click a button.

Image - Bulk Subscribe on  _wp-admin > Users_.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678e9325d624882e7e78ec1f/file-OZlAE1buZl.png)

Last updated on January 31, 2025

### Related Articles

*   [MailChimp shortcodes](/article/1583-mailchimp-shortcodes)
    
*   [Enable double opt-in](/article/1588-enable-double-opt-in)
    
*   [How to sync the user role](/article/1587-how-to-sync-the-user-role)

# MailChimp shortcodes - Ultimate Member

MailChimp shortcodes
====================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Overview
--------

This document provides instructions on how to use shortcodes of the [MailChimp extension](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/mailchimp/)
.

Ultimate Member uses WordPress shortcodes to allow users to perform certain actions as well as display forms and templates. You may use shortcodes in the posts(page) content and text widgets.

**Contents**

*   [\[ultimatemember\_mailchimp\_subscribe\]](#ultimatemember_mailchimp_subscribe)
     - Show subscribe form
*   [\[ultimatemember\_mailchimp\_unsubscribe\]](#ultimatemember_mailchimp_unsubscribe)
     - Show unsubscribe form

Shortcodes
----------

#### \[ultimatemember\_mailchimp\_subscribe\] [#](#ultimatemember_mailchimp_subscribe)

This shortcode used to subscribe users and guests. The shortcode renders a form on the screen. Once the form is submitted the user will be added to the audience with the status “pending”. The user has to confirm the subscription by following the instructions in the email that MailChimp automatically sends to new contacts with the status “pending”.

**Attributes:**

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| _Key_ | _Type_ | _Default_ | _Description_ |
| id  | integer, string | All active connections | The ID of connection, see _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp_.  <br>You can list several connections separated by comma. |
| fields | string | 'user\_email' | The list of fields (metakey) separated by comma. The field 'user\_email' is required. Other required fields added automatically. |
| messages | string | ''  | Custom text shown before the form |
| groups | integer | 0   | Show groups select or not |
| tags | integer | 0   | Show tags select or not |
| autofill | integer | 1   | Autofill form fields with the current user data. |
| show\_checkboxes | integer | 1   | Show audience checkboxes or use hidden inputs. Hidden inputs can’t be turned off. Groups and tags can’t be used without checkboxes. |
| show\_label | integer | 1   | Show field labels or not |
| show\_placeholder | integer | 1   | Show field placeholders or not |
| show\_language | bool | Option value 'Enable language field' | Show language select or not |

**Example** - Subscribe form (full).

`[ultimatemember_mailchimp_subscribe id="34" groups="1" tags="1" show_checkboxes="1" show_label="1" show_placeholder="1",show_language="1"]`

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678f4898409cd453a5fcabb9/file-8YfHsUtJep.png)

**Example** - Subscribe form (compact).

`[ultimatemember_mailchimp_subscribe id="34" show_checkboxes="0" show_label="0"]`

#### ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678f491523073a33451d97cd/file-XA9u447obM.png)

#### \[ultimatemember\_mailchimp\_unsubscribe\] [#](#ultimatemember_mailchimp_unsubscribe)

This shortcode is used to unsubscribe users and guests. The shortcode renders a form on the screen. Once the form is submitted the extension sends the email “MailChimp - Newsletters unsubscribe”. The user has to confirm the unsubscription by clicking a link in the email.

**Attributes:**

|     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| _Key_ | _Type_ | _Default_ | _Description_ |
| id  | integer, string | All active connections | The ID of connection, see _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp_.  <br>You can list several connections separated by comma. |
| messages | string | ''  | Custom text shown before the form |
| autofill | integer | 1   | Autofill form fields with the current user data. |
| show\_checkboxes | integer | 1   | Show audience checkboxes or use hidden inputs. Hidden inputs can’t be turned off. |
| show\_label | integer | 1   | Show field labels or not |
| show\_placeholder | integer | 1   | Show field placeholders or not |

**Example** - Unsubscribe form (full).

`[ultimatemember_mailchimp_unsubscribe]`

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/678f4992b5c1b0711f7150fe/file-3TYlsu0GDd.png)  
**Example** - Unsubscribe form (compact).

`[ultimatemember_mailchimp_unsubscribe show_checkboxes="0" show_label="0"]`

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6797a22ad707d911c05d2e8b/file-N6Lyd2DuDj.png)

Last updated on January 27, 2025

*   [MailChimp Setup](/article/82-mailchimp-setup)

# How to split members into multiple audiences on registration - Ultimate Member

How to split members into multiple audiences on registration
============================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

This article explains how to configure registration forms and MailChimp audience connections to add new members into different audiences depending on their role or choice. 

Please read the article [MailChimp Setup](//docs.ultimatemember.com/article/82-mailchimp-setup)
 first to get know basic information about extension [Ultimate Member - MailChimp](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/mailchimp/)
.

There are two ways to manage newsletter subscriptions on registration: 

*   Automatic audience signup requires user role to be selected on registration;
*   Manual audience signup requires special checkboxes in the registration form.

Automatic audience signup
-------------------------

The simplest way to split your members is to make the registration role-specific and use roles to manage subscribers. Follow these steps:

1) Go to the page \[wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles\] and create user roles. You may use existing UM user roles and role "Subscriber."

![](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pWhXiE8WvhQZ9Ott6uiXG-meArulU7lqJeOlQvST0ZFw1yHh6e5g0ItEYJGy-DV1wunq8Qef2kogYxvenC5epU-zqRYXRxfXacRG_VQCMv3bSbIG5Pjr1l1EAS1QEEPe8BgrxwIl)

2) Go to the page \[wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp\] and create audience connections. You may use a single connection for several roles or individual connections for each role. Select the needed role(s) in the setting "Who can subscribe to this audience." Enable the option "Automatically sync contacts."

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/60ee8b818556b07a2884f8ad/file-9y44EfkoT0.png)

Configure additional settings - groups, tags, fields in the widget **Merge User Meta.**

3) Check up connections on the page \[wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp\]. You should see recently added connections.

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/3XtNrpFHXjLnig9hlGb6PVAqWmq-Z3MpZiTyH6_ekHOtavn-WEDoA275dNtTTbxevCUWIeC65ncMvYTY_SL5Zviqm9MA1H8HBmuoSryVyDhM_R4pMFDY77ZMOfu7P08Shk1lmyp5)

4) Add the field "Roles" to the registration form.  
**Note:** This step is optional. Alternatively, you can create multiple registration forms.

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/DGUrKJTPC6fe1Za1E0ao7zMgXQcvVeZX_aIuFIYebY9mBBFHnWRKEbeY-3WTwI-MmnR3YMOPNe9al9e8POGsdV1EKTi0oFjSySkxabf4425wfKA9anTapqFNutw8XKPd7wgEXaJO)

Test registration form. You should see a role field.

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/p5tiMxnX8ygqPejW-yjoA_sGo07IvDMfR8QOpLPkUjwisMeH1X4sT0Q1lFdVbt-_DNF3dZZIdZN8SnR2h-PabOa663qxcziyEkd_D6qlELLTqk8x-2cGeLk0024J9R16a34G1qST)

Manual audience signup
----------------------

You may allow your members to choose to subscribe to newsletters in the registration form. Follow these steps:

1) Go to the page \[wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp\] and create audience connections. You may use a single connection for several roles or individual connections for each role. Select the needed role(s) in the setting "Who can subscribe to this audience." Disable the option "Automatically sync contacts."

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/60ee8dab00fd0d7c253ffcc7/file-RCJvid1enM.png)

Configure additional settings - groups, tags, fields in the widget Merge User Meta.

2) Check up connections on the page \[wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp\]. You should see recently added connections.

![](https://lh6.googleusercontent.com/yE6c6kKNX8aShNOBOInaO5ume-7eteks0Hpu5Sa-l_zI6uBKzH4Kt8qZbpLJQ-i2bJVCkgHnOX0bVH7DC1240T-atNLTsFmCtNRFr44vrd8OcFa9Vq_j6KBrTfjsOmuyNDfPheHx)

3) Add the field "MailChimp" to the registration form. Add a field for each manual connection.

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/8wuu_S3SLi_urW5aAjTLiYh0a4WBn77R2wdKfbtC_tlbUEIwpStJQl7anROA4zu0p8Aizma0D9GLu94nYdILgbJnCp4gFv36I2vK7y7x-8GgvMHJPqhqKNeFU59JV_zX4bU8Uu08)

Test the registration form. You should see a checkbox for each added field, "MailChimp."

![](https://lh4.googleusercontent.com/fyLZ86vtfaO-0qd8_91YvAA975qeBjaCuCjmAJNLQoE3kDytVyUG_gHBu5TafK96Qz0K157K7Au8x9L7DDaQ-ok18Ntd8JZMuCQWW4Dj3hknREVi7XbKcxoe0Bl7HssnLOkcMYb_)

Last updated on January 4, 2022

### Related Articles

*   [MailChimp Setup](/article/82-mailchimp-setup)
    

No results found

# Enable double opt-in - Ultimate Member

Enable double opt-in
====================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

This article explains how to configure the "double opt-in" feature.

### MailChimp settings

MailChimp audience has the option "Enable double opt-in". Enable this option if you want to send contacts an opt-in confirmation email before subscribe them to the audience.

You also should set options: "Default From name", "Default From email address", "Default Email subject". MailChimp uses these data in the confirmation email.

Image - Audience settings, Audience name and defaults.

![](https://d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5e8643692c7d3a7e9aea484a/file-sc3RdmfCZH.png)

### Ultimate Member settings

Ultimate Member has the option "Enable double opt-in" that can enable the "double opt-in" feature for a certain connection if it is not enabled in the audience settings.

**Note:** this option has lower priority then the setting "Enable double opt-in" in the audience settings.

Image - The connection settings on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > MailChimp > Edit_.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5f8834cb46e0fb001798d418/file-ZobDExtGmS.png)

#### Member approval

The extension adds a contact to the audience after the member's approval. The extension doesn't add new members if their status is pending admin or email verification until these members will be approved. This depends on the "Registration Status" role setting.

Image - User role settings on _wp-admin > Ultimate Member > User Roles > Edit_

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5ed7c1972c7d3a10cba84dd5/file-B3Loevz3eG.png)

The extension adds a contact immediately if the "Auto Approve" option is chosen.

Members should visit their mailbox and click the "Activate your Account" button in the email to approve their accounts if the "Require Email Activation" option is chosen.

Image - Account Activation email example.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6744779e772e5b7dd60d89aa/file-qif0tepluc.png)Administrators should manually approve new members if the "Require Admin Review" option is chosen.

Once the member is approved the extension adds a contact and MailChimp sends the opt-in confirmation email.

Image - Opt-in confirmation email example.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/674478b3772e5b7dd60d89ac/file-zN4q0fmMQS.png)

* * *

Related articles:  
[About Double Opt-In](https://mailchimp.com/help/about-double-opt-in/)
  
[Single Opt-In vs. Double Opt-In](https://mailchimp.com/help/single-opt-in-vs-double-opt-in/)

Last updated on November 25, 2024

### Related Articles

*   [MailChimp Setup](/article/82-mailchimp-setup)
    

No results found
