# Zapier Setup for Triggers - Ultimate Member

Zapier Setup for Triggers
=========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**Overview**
------------

Zapier Triggers are powerful tools for automating workflows in your Ultimate Member (UM) system. A Trigger is an event that starts a Zap, initiating a specific action based on conditions you define. Setting up Triggers allows you to automate tasks like sending notifications, updating profiles, or syncing data across platforms. This guide will walk you through the complete process of setting up Triggers for Ultimate Member using Zapier, enabling you to streamline your processes and save valuable time.

#### Accessing the Private Ultimate Member App on Zapier

Ultimate Member is currently utilizing the following invite link to provide access to our private member app. The app is in the final stages of ensuring full compliance with guidelines. Once reviewed and approved, it will be publicly available and searchable via the App Directory, eliminating the need for an invite link.

To request access to the private app, please use the following invite link: [https://zapier.com/developer/public-invite/156191/af7184f5fb916911bea403d3bf387dd1/](https://zapier.com/developer/public-invite/156191/af7184f5fb916911bea403d3bf387dd1/)

* * *

### What are Zapier Triggers?

Zapier Triggers are events that start a Zap workflow as soon as a condition is met in the source app (e.g., a new row is added in Google Sheets). Triggers are essential to automation, as they pinpoint the exact moment when a task should be carried out, allowing you to efficiently manage repetitive processes.

* * *

### Setup

#### Step 1: Create an Application Password

An application password is a specific type used to authenticate applications or services with your website or system without requiring you to use your main account password. In the Ultimate Member and Zapier integration context, the application password is used to securely connect Zapier to your Ultimate Member account. Follow these steps to create an application password:

1.  Navigate to **WP Admin > Users > Edit a user**.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6732481107276709d1e56b34/file-ly2nrEcJFY.png)
    
2.  Scroll to the **Application Passwords** section and enter a name for the new application password.
3.  Click the **Add New Application Password** button to generate the password.
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67324885268fb643f63a5577/file-uUeFN3gvTP.png)
    
4.  Copy and save the generated password, as you’ll need it later.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/673248f207276709d1e56b35/file-NOcbowywvf.png)
    
5.  Update the profile to save these changes.

* * *

#### Step 2: Set Up Triggers in Zapier

To set up Triggers for Ultimate Member using Zapier, ensure you have a Zapier account. If you don’t yet have an account, sign up on the [Zapier website](https://zapier.com/)
. Once you’re logged in, follow the steps below to configure triggers with Ultimate Member.

1.  **Log in to Zapier**
    *   Visit the Zapier [login page](https://zapier.com/app/login)
         and enter your credentials to log in.
2.  **Create a New Zap**
    *   Once logged in, click on the **\+ Create** button on the Zapier dashboard and select **Zaps** to create a new Zap. Alternatively, you can click Zap from the home tab.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67324a41442d517d60d26697/file-Azl7TJWthq.png)
3.  **Choose Ultimate Member as the Trigger App**
    *   In the **Zap Trigger Settings**, name your Zap then click on **Trigger** to select the event that will start your Zap.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67324ad5442d517d60d26698/file-xEDuhAuQXT.png)
    *   Search for **Ultimate Member (1.0.0)** in the app list and select it as your trigger app.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67324b171f08a96042002c73/file-wasagRlveS.png)
    *   **Connect Your Ultimate Member Account**:
        *   In the **Setup** tab, located within the step details on the right-hand side, select **New User Activity** as the Trigger Event.  
            ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67324b8f442d517d60d2669a/file-mOKm2RIxZ3.png)
        *   In the **Account** option, click **Select**, and choose an existing account, or click **+Connect a new account**. This will open a new tab for the account connection or authentication prompt.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67324c47442d517d60d2669c/file-mN4gpglGku.png)
        *   Enter your **website URL**, **username or email address**, and the **Application Password** you created.
        *   Click **Yes, Continue to Ultimate Member (1.0.0)** button to authenticate.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6798bebbcf2b676f9d8cfdf0/file-ZD0XKSPFd6.png)
    *   After a successful connection, you’ll be redirected back to Zapier. Click the **Continue** button.
4.  **![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6798bf492c3e3943f85cd404/file-pzLqLewA3d.png)Complete the Trigger Setup**
    *   Follow the steps to finish configuring the trigger in Ultimate Member or refer to the "Set Up the Trigger in Ultimate Member" guide within this article for detailed instructions. ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6798c0412c3e3943f85cd408/file-Y6EWpgn23y.png)
    *   Once you've finished setting up the trigger in Ultimate Member, go back to your Zap in Zapier and click "Continue" to test your trigger and confirm that it's set up correctly.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67324ed4442d517d60d266a1/file-s044klcOKx.png)

* * *

### Setup the Trigger in Ultimate Member

The instructions below explain how to configure the Trigger within Ultimate Member for Zapier. For more information about each setting, please refer to this [documentation.](//docs.ultimatemember.com/category/1731-zapier)

1.  **Create a Zap in Ultimate Member**
    *   Go to **WP Admin > Zapier > Zaps** and click **Add new zap**.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67324fd3442d517d60d266a2/file-9dJT5tpmf3.png)
2.  **Configure the Zap Settings**
    *   **Name Your Zap**: For example, "New User Register."
    *   Under **Setup Zaps**, choose **Trigger** as your Zap type and set **Status** to **Running**.
3.  **Specify the Trigger Event and Conditions**
    *   In **Trigger Settings**, select a trigger event (e.g., **When a new user registers**). This will push data to the Zapier Webhook when the event occurs.
    *   **Role Settings**: Choose specific roles to control which users’ data will be sent to Zapier. If you leave it blank, data for all users, regardless of role, will be sent.  
        
        **Note:** You can select specific roles to filter which users’ registration data is sent to Zapier. If you choose one or more roles, only the registration data of users with those selected roles will be pushed to Zapier. If no roles are selected, the registration data of all users will be sent, regardless of their roles.
        
    *   **Fields Setting**: Select the specific fields to include in the webhook request (e.g., `first_name`, `last_name`, `birth_date`, user\_email.)
4.  **Save and Test the Trigger**
    *   Click **Send Data to Zapier** and then **Create** to save.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/675744bac83f0231bca46d07/file-CnXT7DXtyc.png)
    *   Go back to the **Zaps** in Zapier and test the trigger to verify that the data flows correctly.

* * *

### Testing the Trigger

1.  In the **Configure** tab in the Zapier step details, click the **Test trigger** button to ensure that data is flowing correctly from Ultimate Member to Zapier. When a new user registers, the trigger should display the data from the selected fields.  
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6798dcd6390edd6090f44017/file-qn3fIQgPn7.png)
2.  In the Test tab, click **Find New Records** to fetch the latest data from Ultimate Member. Select the most recent record displayed.
3.  Then click **Continue with Selected Record** to proceed to set up the Action for this Zap. You’ll see the new records in the **Test** tab, with the fields from Ultimate Member successfully synchronized with Zapier.

### Set Up the Action in Zapier

To set up an Action (the response to the Trigger), you’ll configure Google Sheets as the destination for the user registration data.

1.  **Select Google Sheets as the Action App**
    *   Search for **Google Sheets** in the application list and select it.  
        ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6732558e268fb643f63a5587/file-R4gAvAQ0au.png)
2.  **Choose an Event**
    *   In the **Setup** tab, within the step details on the right-hand side, go to the **Action Event** section and select an event (e.g., **Create Spreadsheet Row**) to define the action that will be executed when the Zap runs.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67574651c003e400f6461fee/file-MCBV64ZUgs.png)
3.  **Connect Your Google Sheets Account**
    *   In the **Account** option, choose the account you want to connect, or connect a new account, then click **Continue**.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67574d87c003e400f6461ff4/file-Hakub392LM.png)
4.  **Configure Action Settings**
    *   In the **Configure** tab:
        *   Select the **Drive**, **Spreadsheet**, and **Worksheet** you want to use.
        *   Select the data fields configured in the Ultimate Member Trigger. These fields determine where the registration data will be stored in Google Sheets. Once the fields are mapped.
        *   Click the **Continue** button to proceed with testing and confirm that the data will be transferred correctly.  
            ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/675752b2513c544d5dbadd26/file-gVF88NVzyu.png)
5.  **Test the Action**
    *   In the **Test** tab, review the fields displayed under **Data In**. 
    *   Click the **Test step** button to confirm that the data from Ultimate Member is being correctly transferred to Google Sheets.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6757549dc003e400f6462007/file-eUnc1k7rUC.png)
    *   Once successful, you’ll be notified that data has been sent to Google Sheets.   
        ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/675756bac83f0231bca46d17/file-PB9ylo54gU.png)
    *   Check your spreadsheet to confirm that the data has appeared.
6.  **Publish Your Zap**
    *   Click **Publish** to save and activate your Zap. This step ensures synchronization between Ultimate Member and Google Sheets for every new registration on your site.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67576635513c544d5dbadd40/file-ZVOStOvt7W.png)
    *   Once your Zap is published, navigate to **WP Admin > Ultimate Member > Zapier** and edit the relevant trigger Zap. To synchronize data from Ultimate Member (UM) to Google Sheets, follow these steps:  
        
        1.  Click the **Send Data to Zapier** button.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67576855c003e400f646201a/file-6qcg4xVJwU.png)
        2.  A popup alert will appear displaying the **Webhook URL**. This URL acts as the connection point between Ultimate Member and Zapier, allowing data to flow between the two systems.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67576863b7cbdc1903bfb809/file-Gx4yuvcb85.png)
        3.  Copy the Webhook URL and paste it into the **Zap Webhook URL** field in your Zap settings within Zapier.
        4.  Click the **Save** button to save the changes.
        
        This ensures that your Zap is correctly configured to receive data from Ultimate Member and sync it with Google Sheets.
        
    *   Whenever a new user is created or registers on your Ultimate Member site, their data is automatically sent to Google Sheets.  
        ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67576a0ec83f0231bca46d30/file-eFpv9mPIAe.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/67576a03c83f0231bca46d2f/file-6JKn4DDPE2.png)

* * *

By following these detailed steps, you’ll have a fully automated workflow set up in Zapier to handle new registrations on your Ultimate Member site, sending data directly to Google Sheets or another application of your choice.

Last updated on January 31, 2025

No results found

# Installing a Theme - Ultimate Member

Installing a Theme
==================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Instruction on How to install a theme from ZIP archive:

*   go to the page _\[wp-admin > Appearance > Themes\]_;
*   Click the **"Add New"** button;
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a72bbad3efbe495c3b4184/file-VUFaclkaxr.png)
    
*   Click the **"Upload Theme"** button;
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a72c33efc78d0553e5f17e/file-DKbRXm0XyI.png)
    
*   Click the **"Choose File"** button - an additional window should appear;  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a72c8d663c7a603de8dbfb/file-64SXOPCgIO.png)
    
*   select an archive with the theme;  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a72cc064e42a671b63be7b/file-R8aqKsKexb.png)
    
*   Click the **"Install Now"** button - the installation will start;  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a72d0c64e42a671b63be7c/file-V2FBEn8iJt.png)

# UM Theme Sidebars - Ultimate Member

UM Theme Sidebars
=================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

UM, Theme has 6 Sidebar areas. This means you display widgets in 6 areas. These are:

1.  Content Sidebar
2.  Profile Sidebar
3.  Footer Column 1
4.  Footer Column 2
5.  Footer Column 3
6.  Footer Column 4

**\[wp-admin> Widgets\]**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61aa16a528c7c85868bec550/file-Rq3cvNrxrd.png)

**How to Disable sidebar Areas**

In UM Theme you can disable or enable the Sidebar areas separately. For example, if you want to display a sidebar in Posts but not in Pages, you can do that easily from Appearance > Customize > Content > Sidebar Management > Sidebar Location.

You can enable or disable Sidebar areas for the following post-types & plugins. 

1.  Single Post
2.  Pages
3.  Archive Pages
4.  Search Page
5.  [Ultimate Member](https://wordpress.org/plugins/ultimate-member/)
    : Group Pages
6.  WooCommerce: Shop Page
7.  WooCommerce: Product Page
8.  [ForumWP](https://forumwpplugin.com/)
    : Forum Pages
9.  [ForumWP](https://forumwpplugin.com/)
    : Topic Pages
10.  [ForumWP](https://forumwpplugin.com/)
    : Reply Pages
11.  [ForumWP](https://forumwpplugin.com/)
    : Tag Pages
12.  [ForumWP](https://forumwpplugin.com/)
    : Category Pages  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61aa19a58564e02763629533/file-72y64KrNYt.png)![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61aa19b628c7c85868bec562/file-fuOinOcFOm.png)

# Disable Page Title on ALL Pages - Ultimate Member

Disable Page Title on ALL Pages
===============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

In UM Theme, you can remove the page title from individual pages by assigning the " **Page Builder**" template from Page Attributes ( _Dashboard > Pages > Select the page > Page Attributes_). But if you want to remove the page title from all pages, you have to use the hooks to remove title from all the pages. Or you can use [WordPress Conditional Tags](https://codex.wordpress.org/Conditional_Tags)
 to selectively remove the titles.

remove\_action( 'um\_theme\_page', 'um\_theme\_output\_page\_header' );

**NOTE: We have created this code example to provide guidance and to make it easier for you to implement this code into your website. However, we are not able to provide any support when it comes to customizing the theme. If you need help implementing this code, please hire a developer.**

Last updated on November 26, 2018

# Customizing Header Section - Ultimate Member

Customizing Header Section
==========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b054f88564e0276362a7ef/file-ijaaFd4zkB.png)

**How To Change the Header Background Color**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Style**
2.  Click on the **Header Background Color** color picker to change the background color.
3.  After making the changes click on **Publish**.

**How To Increase or decrease the Header Padding**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Style**
2.  Write your desired padding value ( _10px_ ) in **Site Header Padding** Field.
3.  After making the changes click on **Publish**.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5bbad9be042863158cc7260c/file-yFFdvR5QJv.png)

# bbPress Forum Header Color - Ultimate Member

bbPress Forum Header Color
==========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

Follow these steps to change the bbPress forum header color:

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > bbPress**
2.  Click on **bbPress Colors**
3.  Use the following color picker to change the header color:  
      
    Forum Header Background Color  
    Forum Header Text Color  
    Forum Header Border Color
4.  Click on **Publish** to save your changes.

**Note:** This document is intended for theme customization and not for the bbPress extension.

# um_theme_header - Ultimate Member

um\_theme\_header
=================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**Hook Type**: Action

**Description**: Add some content between the <header> tag.

**Currently Hooked Function**:

um\_theme\_header\_skip\_links()
um\_theme\_header\_custom\_background()
um\_theme\_core\_header()

**Usage**:

add\_action( 'um\_theme\_header', 'um\_theme\_core\_header' );

**Example**:

<?php
add\_action( 'um\_theme\_header', 'my\_theme\_header' );

function my\_theme\_header() { 
	// your code here;
 } 
?>

**Change log**: Since 1.0

**Source**: is located in header.php of line 38

Last updated on November 19, 2018

# Footer Bottom Bar - Ultimate Member

Footer Bottom Bar
=================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

You can display anything you want in the Footer. To do that, go to **Appearance > Customize > Footer > Footer – Bottom Bar.**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a89ef979d5be1f25dd4ebf/file-Z3c22FRxnW.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a89b4364e42a671b63c637/file-DhIF2APJTH.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a89f60663c7a603de8e3a5/file-wLxn9SfYw6.png)

In this section, you can display both Footer Menu & Any texts in 1 or 2 columns depending on your need. Set the Footer Bar layout from **Appearance > Customize > Footer > Footer Bottom Bar > Footer Bar Layout**.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5bbad6c7042863158cc725f8/file-0raKGbCpdX.png)

**Changing Footer Section Color**

*   Footer Text - Changes the color of footer Text.
*   Footer Link - Changes the color of the footer link.
*   Footer Link Hover - Changes the color of the footer hover.
*   Footer Background - Changes the color of the footer background.
*   Footer Menu -  Changes the color of the footer menu.   
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61a8a1d7efc78d0553e5f924/file-5Md0fhsTDz.png)

Changing bbPress Sticky Topic Color
===================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > bbPress**
2.  Click on **bbPress Colors**
3.  Click on **Sticky topic background color** to set the color.
4.  Click on **Publish** to save your changes.

# Single Article - Ultimate Member

Single Article
==============

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How To Change the Single Post Layout & Color**

In UM Theme you can change the single article layout & color by going to **Appearance > Customize > Content > Post Layout**. There you will be presented with the below options:

**Color**

*   _Article Box Color_: Single article background color.
*   _Article Text Color_: Single article content color.
*   _Post Tag Background Color_:  Single post tag background color.
*   _Post Tag Color_:  Single post tag color. 
*   ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61aa24bf48287b4578808b85/file-tPvDoHRewf.png)
    

**Show or Hide Single-Article Items**  

*   _Show Article Summary:_ Enable or disable
*   _Post Navigation_: Hide Next / Previous post section
*   _Post Tags_: Remove posts tags.
*   _Post Category_: Remove post category
*   _Author Box_: Remove Author details.
*   _Comments_: Remove the article comment box.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61aa292c48287b4578808baa/file-JCLoRTtohl.png)
    

**Appearance > Customize > Content > Sidebar Management**

**Sidebar**

*   _Sidebar Width_: Select the width of a single article page sidebar.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61aa27eede3d7f58bfc6aab2/file-U1Y70Clwvt.png)
    

Last updated on December 9, 2021

### Related Articles

*   [Changing Website Width](/article/1372-changing-website-width)
    
*   [Post Archive](/article/1384-post-archive)

# Display Social Media Icons in the Header Top Bar - Ultimate Member

Display Social Media Icons in the Header Top Bar
================================================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

### Overview

In UM Theme, you can display the icon linked to 6 preferred Social Media Accounts. You can select from a wide range of Social Media Icons (61 Icons) and assign them using the WordPress Customizer. After setting up links, they will appear on the **Header Top bar.  
**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6220e769c1688a6d26a718a6/file-irXGHuJbuf.png)

**How to Display Social Media Icon**

Here are the steps to display social media icons on your website:

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > General Styles > Social Account.  
    **
2.  Select the _icon_ from the dropdown and insert the link to your social media profile below the dropdown.
3.  After including all your Social Media Profiles, click on **Publish**.![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6220e81bc1e53608cf9e4b46/file-Co8fNFTv1Y.png)

**How To Change Social Media Icons Color**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > General Styles > Social Accounts**
2.  Click on Social Icon Color & Social Icon Hover color picker to change the color.
3.  Click on **Publish** to save your changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/6220e8c22ce7ed0fb09119f2/file-F41O9INYF4.png)

Last updated on March 3, 2022

No results found

# Post Archive - Ultimate Member

Post Archive
============

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How To Change the Post Archive, Category & Tag page posts layout**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Content > Post Archive**

3.  Change the position of the sidebar using the Sidebar Positioning dropdown.
4.  Use **Post background Color** & **Post Content Color** picker to set the color.
5.  Click on **Publish** to save your changes.

# Changing Website Width - Ultimate Member

Changing Website Width
======================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How To Change the Website Width**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > General Styles > Container Width**
2.  Write your website width in px or % (example: 1200px or 90%) in **Site Width** field.
3.  Click on **Publish** to save your changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5bbadae3042863158cc72614/file-CfpDsbEo3Y.png)

# Customizing Topbar Color & Font - Ultimate Member

Customizing Topbar Color & Font
===============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61acba2d7a3b8c03913d41a3/file-X35ubcIizv.png)

**How To Change the Topbar Background Color**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Topbar Style**
2.  Click on the **Top Bar Background** color picker to change the color
3.  Click on **Publish** to save the changes.

**How To Change the Topbar Menu Color**

1.   Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Topbar Style**
2.  Click on the **Top Bar Link** color picker to change the color
3.  Click on **Publish** to save the changes.

**How To Change the Topbar Text Color**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Topbar Style**
2.  Click on the **Top Bar Text Color** color picker to change the color
3.  Click on **Publish** to save the changes.

**How To Change the Topbar Content Font Size**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Top Bar Style**
2.  Write your desired font size in the **Topbar Font Size** field.
3.  You can write your font size in px, em, or rem ( _example: 16px_ )
4.  Click on **Publish** to save the changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5c6a3fc82c7d3a66e32e9110/file-LiTnZnr0e7.png)

# Customizing Form Fields Color - Ultimate Member

Customizing Form Fields Color
=============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How To Change the color of Form Fields**

You can change the style and color of all Buttons globally from **Appearance > Customize > General Styles > Form Fields**.

Here is a brief explanation of all options:

*            **Form Border:** Form fields border color.
*            **Form Text:** Form field text color.
*            **Form Field Background:** Form fields background color.
*            **Form Border Focus:** Form fields border focus color.
*            **Form Error Text:** Form error text color.
*            **Form Error Background:** Form error text background color.
*            **Form Field Label:** Form filed label color.

# Setting up Header Menu - Ultimate Member

Setting up Header Menu
======================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How to set the Header Primary Menu**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Menus.**
2.  Click on **create a new menu** from the top.
3.  Give your menu a name in the **Menu Name** field and click on **Create Menu.**
4.  On the left, you will find all pages & posts are listed. Select the item you want in your menu and click on **Add to menu**
5.  You will see **Menu Settings** under Menu structure.
6.  Check the tick mark beside **Primary Menu.**
7.  Click on **Save Menu  
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61adbf7b4fba243f1f841df4/file-DRvOaDmmEZ.png)**

**How To Change the Color, Font size of Header Primary menu**

To customize the color of the primary header menu, Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Menu.**

Here is a detailed explanation of all the header menu customization options:

##### **Menu Font Settings**

*   **Menu Font:** Change your header menu font and select from a vast list of Google Fonts.
*   **Menu Font Size:** Write your menu font size in px, em or rem (_example: 10px_)
*   **Menu Alignment:** Align your menu to the left, center, or right.
*   **Menu Font Weight**: Select the thickness of your menu text.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61adc0e97a3b8c03913d433d/file-HMZbbvptzh.png)
    

**Submenu Settings**

*   **Submenu Arrow:** Hide or show the arrow indication beside the sub-menu.
*   **Submenu Color**: Change your Submenu color.
*   **Submenu Text Color:** Change your Submenu text color.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61adc1214fba243f1f841dfa/file-6mKvXcW9Ds.png)
    

**Menu Colors**

*   **Menu Item Color:** Change your menu text color.
*   **Menu Hover Color:** Select the color of the menu text if the user hovers over it.
*   **Selected Menu Text Color:** Change the Menu Text-color once selected.
*   **Selected Menu Background Color:** Select what will be the color of the menu background once  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61adc1a548287b4578809265/file-XfscPcK4ah.png)
    

Last updated on July 3, 2023

### Related Articles

*   [Setting up User Header Menu](/article/1868-setting-up-user-header-menu)
    

No results found

# How To Enable Header Topbar - Ultimate Member

How To Enable Header Topbar
===========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**Front-end:**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5b5ff9a42c7d3a03f89d2be1/file-5pxDvQ2gLW.png)

**Back-end:**

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5b85249f0428631d7a8a7319/file-Rdmelogc9v.png)

In UM Theme you can display  Menu, Social Links, or any text at your topbar. Follow these steps to enable the top bar in the Header:

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Topbar Layout**
2.  In the drop-down, you will find 3 options ( _None, 1 Column & 2 Column_ )
3.  If you want to display your content in 1 column, select **1 Column**
4.  Wait for the customizer section auto-reload, then you will see **Column 1 Layout** Dropdown
5.  You can display Text, Social Media Icons, or Menu (_Header Top Menu – accessible from Appearance > Menus_).
6.  If you want to display your header content in 2 Column, select 2 Column from Top bar Layout Dropdown.
7.  Click on **Publish** after making your changes.

Last updated on December 5, 2021

### Related Articles

*   [Customizing Topbar Color & Font](/article/1349-customizing-topbar)
    
*   [Change Header Topbar layout](/article/1350-header-topbar-layout)
    

No results found

# Setting up Header Logo - Ultimate Member

Setting up Header Logo
======================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5b5ffd210428631d7a897bfb/file-qz7jS8NBHl.jpg)

In UM Theme you can either upload your own image logo or use text as a logo. Follow these steps to upload your logo in the header section.

**How to upload logo image in Header**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Logo**
2.  Select your logotype (text or Image Logo) from the **Header Logo Type** dropdown.
3.   If you select Image Logo, then upload your logo image by clicking the **Select Image** button
4.  Suggested image dimension for logo is _150px x 150px_
5.  After the upload is finished, click on **Publish**.  
      
    _Note: Logo Image- Retina must be a higher resolution logo image for (higher pixel density) screens to have a better user experience._
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61adbc034fba243f1f841dec/file-6iQMZEwf5Z.png)
    

**How To Change the Header Logo Font**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Logo**
2.  Select your logotype _text_ from the **Header Logo Type** dropdown.
3.  Write your text in the **Site Title** field.
4.  Change the text color using the **Logo Color** color picker.
5.  Change the logo text font family from the Logo Font dropdown.
6.  You can change the Logo Font size & weight from Logo Font Size & Logo Font Weight option.
7.  After making your changes click on **Publish**.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5bbad89a042863158cc72607/file-hFFXJENsRW.png)

Last updated on March 16, 2023

No results found

# Customizing Heading Text & Font - Ultimate Member

Customizing Heading Text & Font
===============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How To Change the Font of Heading Text**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > General Styles > Headings**
2.  Select your desired _font_ from the **Heading Font** dropdown.
3.  Change the text color of heading text using **Heading Text Color** picker.
4.  Change font format & weight using the **Font Format** & **Font Weight** options.
5.  Click on **Publish** after making the changes.

**How To Change the Font Size of Heading Text**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > General Styles > Headings**
2.  Write your desired font size in **H1 Font Size** _to_ the **H6 Font Size** field.
3.  Click on **Publish** after making the changes. 

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b0695648287b4578809e8d/file-7UNnI1xsTN.png)

# Setting up Search Box in Header - Ultimate Member

Setting up Search Box in Header
===============================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How to show Search Box in Header**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Search**
2.  Select **Enable** from **Header-Search** Dropdown. ( Enable for all, Enable for logged in users and Disable)
3.  Click on **Publish**.

**Set the Type of Search box**

*   **Member Search:** Will search the keyword to find only the Member Profiles that match the search term.
*   **Search All Content:** Will search from the whole website.

**How To Change the Header Search Box Color**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Search**
2.  Click on the **Search Box Color** color picker to change the search box color.
3.  Use the **Search Text Color** & **Placeholder Text Color** to change the search text and placeholder text color.
4.  After making the changes click on **Publish**.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5b852b762c7d3a03f89e3079/file-VPinwGEkNL.png)

# Header - Logged Out - Ultimate Member

Header - Logged Out
===================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5b5fff7d0428631d7a897c16/file-RnPlCzmqpV.jpg)

If a user is logged out then you can either display them a text or display buttons to direct them to your desired page.

**Setting the Button for logged out users**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header – Logged Out**
2.  Select either button or text from **What to display to logged-out users**.

*   Show Button 1 & 2
*   Show Button 1
*   Show Button 2
*   Show Text

3\. If you select Button, you will be presented with the below options:

*   _Button Text_: Your buttons text
*   _Button URL_: Link of the Button
*   _Button Color_: Whole Button Color
*   _Button Text Color_: Text color of the button.
*   _Button Hover Color_: Button Hover background color.
*   _Button Hover Text Color_: Color of text when hovering over.
*   _Button Border_: Enable or disable button border.

  
4\. Click on **Publish** to save your changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5bbad947042863158cc7260a/file-WB6XgQCydP.png)

Last updated on December 2, 2021

No results found

# UM Theme LifterLMS Support - Ultimate Member

UM Theme LifterLMS Support
==========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

UM Theme is fully compatible with the powerful learning management system plugin [LifterLMS](https://wordpress.org/plugins/lifterlms/)
. When you have LifterLMS installed you will see the color customization options for LifterLMS buttons, pricing tables, and checkout section.

To customize LifterLMS colors go to **Appearance > Customize > LifterLMS**. There you will find below tabs:

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5bb9fb69042863158cc723aa/file-3EowJoD7AX.png)

*   **LifterLMS Buttons**: Change the color of LifterLMS primary & secondary buttons.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b1deff77c88e1adaf5fbad/file-jZkTuueFdV.png)
    
*   **LifterLMS Pricing Tables**: Color of pricing plan section.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b1df22ed6813742673ff4b/file-8ZkCxuGerf.png)
    
*   **LifterLMS Checkout**: Change the color of checkout page elements.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b1dfc097682b790dad32c3/file-uAXKfamGPW.png)
    

Last updated on December 9, 2021

No results found

# Change Header Topbar layout - Ultimate Member

Change Header Topbar layout
===========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How To Display the Top Bar Content in 2 Columns**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Top Bar Layout**
2.  Select **2 Column** from **Top Bar Layout**
3.  You can show below items in each Column  
      
    
    **Text**: Write your text in the text field.  
    **Text & Social Icons**: Display both Text & Social Media Icons  
    **Menu**: Topbar Menu ( setup from Appearance > Menus)  
    **Menu & Social Icons**: Both Social Icons & Menu  
    **Social Icons**: Social Media Icons ( Setup from _Appearance > Customize > General Styles > Social Accounts_)
    
4.  Select what type of content you want to show in each column.
5.  Click **Publish** to save your changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b057d18564e0276362a7f3/file-mFprxLuhzP.png)

Last updated on December 8, 2021

### Related Articles

*   [Customizing Topbar Color & Font](/article/1349-customizing-topbar)

# Header Notifications - Ultimate Member

Header Notifications
====================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/5c6a51ea042863543ccd1b2c/file-ikdFh3AkW3.gif)

In UM Theme, you can check out your recent notifications from the header section. For that, you need to have the Ultimate Member [Real-Time Notifications extension](https://ultimatemember.com/extensions/real-time-notifications/)
 installed.

### How to display Real-Time Notifications in Header

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize**
2.  Then go to **Header > Header Notification Modal**
3.  Select Enable from **Show Header Notification** dropdown.
4.  Click on **Publish** to save changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61ac9c5c64dede799a106c82/file-T7fwhaTjsJ.png)

### To Change the Notification Icon Color

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Notification Modal**
2.  Click on the Notification Icon Color picker to change the icon color.
    
3.  Click on the  Notification Hover Color to change the hover color.
    
4.  Click on **Publish** to save your changes.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61acadef8564e02763629ab3/file-LQniaaEtqb.png)
    

### Customizing the color of Notification Modal

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Notification Modal**
2.  You can customize the colors of _**Notification Bubble Color, Notification Counter Color, Notification Box Color, Notification Text Color**_ by selecting from the color picker.  
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61acaf458564e02763629ab5/file-UOOTrWSixi.png)
    
3.  Click on **Publish** to save your changes.

Last updated on December 5, 2021

No results found

# Meta Information Color - Ultimate Member

Meta Information Color
======================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How to Change the Website Meta Information Color**

Meta Information is the supporting text of the website. For example, article publishing time & date, article tag, comment reply link, and post author name fall under Meta information. 

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b1f9efed6813742673ffe3/file-JRZkLs5k9h.png)

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > General Styles > SiteWide Color**
2.  Click on **Meta Information Color** picker to change the color.
3.  Click on **Publish** to save the changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b207e01fc2de68e204f168/file-qnB3wKWguH.png)

# Setting up User Header Menu - Ultimate Member

Setting up User Header Menu
===========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

The **User Header Menu** is shown when you hover a mouse on the user name (user avatar) in the top-right corner of the screen. 

The User Header Menu displays all available pages if empty. You have to create a menu for this area.

Follow these steps:

1.  Go to _wp-admin > Appearance > Menus_.
2.  Click the **create a new menu** link from the top.
3.  Give your menu a name in the **Menu Name** field.
4.  Check the **User Header Menu** item in the **Menu Settings** section.
5.  Click the **Create Menu** button.
6.  On the left, you will find all pages and posts are listed. Select items you want in your menu and click the **Add to menu** button.
7.  Click the **Save Menu** button to save changes.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64a29371b71911316e4003f6/file-VzsYEgpsph.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64a29386b71911316e4003f7/file-zuHkGt3tjY.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64a293a41f9ba00c2bcf9a20/file-dr5YQx4u6X.png)

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64a293b7f39eb10e8e84bc08/file-pSTavUfyts.png)

Once the User Header Menu is created you'll see it under the user name (user avatar) on hover.

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/64a293eeb71911316e4003f8/file-afX2CpvyJF.png)

Last updated on July 3, 2023

### Related Articles

*   [Setting up Header Menu](/article/1352-setting-up-header-menu)
    

No results found

# Extension: Private Messages - Ultimate Member

Extension: Private Messages
===========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

**How To change the Private Messages Color**

You need to have the Private Messages extension installed to use this section. Go to **Appearance > Customize > Ultimate Member > Private Messages**. There you will be presented with the below options:

1.  **Message Color**: Set the Message text color.
2.  **Message Background Color**:  Set the Message background color.
3.  **Message Button Text Color**: Change the Send Message Button text color.
4.  **Message Button Color**:  Change the Send Message Button color.
5.  **Message Layout**: Set Message section layout.

======================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

 extension installed.

2.  Select **Enable** from Show Header Messenger dropdown
3.  Click on **Publish  
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61ac92b448287b45788090b9/file-Fe0qOEMwpJ.png)**

### **To Change the Message Icon Color**

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Header Messenger Modal**
2.  Click on the Message Icon Color picker to change the icon color.

4.  Click on **Publish** to save your changes.  
    
    ![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61ac936464dede799a106c77/file-SRuXu6BL7v.png)

    _Message Box Color

# Enable Header Bottom Bar - Ultimate Member

Enable Header Bottom Bar
========================

[](javascript:window.print() "Print this article")

![](//d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/561c96629033600a7a36d662/images/61b30514ed6813742674047f/file-PDlpLkdqm2.png)

In UM Theme you can display Bottom Bar with Menu, Social Links, or any text. Follow these steps to enable Bottom Bar in Header:

1.  Go to **Appearance > Customize > Header > Bottom Bar Layout**
2.  In the **Bottom Bar** drop down you will find 3 options ( Enable, Disable & Visible on Click )  
      
    **Enable**: Displays the Bottom Bar
    
    **Disable**: No Bottom Bar  
    **Visible on Click**: Will stay hidden but will appear when clicked
    
3.  Click on **Publish** after making your changes.

Last updated on December 10, 2021
